items.note: Pick one mood: futur II for a real future plan, conditional for a daydream — never both at once.

activities.title: Edit your own text
activities.intro: Editor mode: every question here is an error you might have just written. Catch them now and your partner will have nothing left to correct.

questions.prompt: A male speaker says 'I would come'. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: ja → bih, and a male speaker uses the masculine participle došao. došla would be a female speaker.

questions.prompt: A real plan for tomorrow — which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Plan → futur I: ću + infinitive. With Sutra first, ću must come second — 'Sutra radit ću' breaks the clitic rule, and 'ću da radim' isn't Croatian.

questions.prompt: Pomažem ___ .
questions.explanation: pomagati governs the dative: roditeljima. And because the subject owns them, svoj is the right possessive — svojim roditeljima.

questions.prompt: Complete the wish: 'Htio ___ tečno govoriti hrvatski.'
questions.explanation: Wish → conditional: Htio bih tečno govoriti hrvatski. The participle opens the sentence, bih follows in second position.

questions.prompt: Turn the fact into a wish: 'Volim raditi od kuće.' → 'Najviše ___ ___ raditi od kuće.' (male speaker)
questions.explanation: Najviše bih volio raditi od kuće. — najviše opens, so bih takes second position, then the participle volio.

questions.prompt: Fix the clitic order and write the whole corrected sentence: 'Javit ću se ti sutra.'
questions.explanation: Dative before se: Javit ću ti se sutra. The chain is aux (ću) → dative (ti) → se.

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'If I had more free time, I would call my brother more often.'
questions.answer: Kad bih imao više slobodnog vremena, češće bih zvao brata.
questions.explanation: više slobodnog vremena — quantity + genitive. In the result clause, češće opens and bih again takes second position.

questions.prompt: Kupit ću ___ poklon. (for my wife — the recipient)
questions.explanation: The recipient goes in the dative: svojoj ženi. svoju ženu would be the accusative — that would make her the thing being bought!

days.title: Transport i komunikacija — getting around
days.objective: Command the vocabulary of transport and travel logistics.
days.paretoFocus: vlak, tramvaj, presjedanje, red vožnje, kašnjenje — transport words plus your case system handle tickets, delays and directions anywhere in Croatia.
drills: Do Unit 29 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 transport words: kolodvor, peron, polazak, dolazak, presjedanje, karta u jednom smjeru…
Say how you get to work/town: instrumental of means (tramvajem, autom, pješice).
Ask 3 timetable questions: Kada polazi…? S kojeg perona…?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Buy a return ticket to Rijeka in a role-play.
Say your commute in one sentence with instrumental.

activities.title: Na kolodvoru — 14 transport words
activities.intro: The station vocabulary that runs every trip in Croatia. Learn these fourteen and you can read any departures board and survive any ticket window. Watch two old friends inside the list: the fleeting a (polazak → polaska) and sibilarization (putnik → putnici).

items.en: (train or bus) station
items.note: glavni kolodvor = the main train station; autobusni kolodvor = bus station.

items.en: platform
items.note: S kojeg perona…? = from which platform (s + genitive).

items.en: departure
items.note: Fleeting a: polazak → polaska (G sg.). Polasci = the departures board.

items.en: arrival
items.note: Same family as dolaziti (to come/arrive).

items.en: transfer, changing (trains/buses)
items.note: From presjedati; the perfective verb is presjesti: Morate presjesti u Ogulinu.

items.en: timetable
items.note: Literally 'order of driving' — vožnje is a genitive.

items.en: delay
items.note: Vlak ima kašnjenje = vlak kasni — both mean the train is late.

items.en: one-way ticket
items.note: smjer = direction; u jednom smjeru = in one direction (locative).

items.en: return ticket
items.note: From povratak (return) — the cheaper way to travel twice.

items.en: train, bus, tram
items.note: Your three everyday vehicles — all masculine.

items.en: stop (bus, tram)
items.note: autobusna stanica; tramvajsko stajalište — you'll hear both.

items.en: driver
items.note: Soft č → instrumental vozačem, not 'vozačom'.

items.en: passenger, traveller
items.note: Plural putnici — k → c before -i, sibilarization strikes again.

items.en: luggage
items.note: Always singular in Croatian, like 'luggage' in English.

activities.title: Tramvajem, autom, pješice — how do you get around?
activities.intro: Means of transport go in the instrumental with NO preposition: tramvajem, vlakom, autom. (English says 'by tram'; Croatian just declines.) The one exception: pješice (on foot) is an adverb — no case at all. Then come the three timetable questions you'll actually ask.

items.en: I go to work by tram.
items.note: tramvaj ends in soft j → instrumental -em: tramvajem.

items.en: I travel by train.
items.note: Hard consonant → -om: vlakom.

items.en: Are you going by car or by bus?
items.note: Foreign noun auto declines like a masculine: auta, autu, autom.

items.en: I ride a bicycle.
items.note: voziti se = to ride — don't forget the se.

items.en: I'm flying to London by plane.
I go on foot.
items.note: pješice (also pješke) is an adverb — the one 'means of transport' with no instrumental.

items.en: When does the train to Rijeka leave?
items.note: Destination: za + accusative — vlak za Rijeku.

items.hr: S kojeg perona polazi vlak za Split?
items.en: From which platform does the train to Split leave?
items.note: s + genitive: s kojeg perona, s perona broj dva.

items.hr: Koliko košta povratna karta za Zadar?
items.en: How much is a return ticket to Zadar?

items.hr: Vlak polazi u osam i deset s perona broj dva.
items.en: The train leaves at 8:10 from platform two.
items.note: Your answer template: time (u + A), then platform (s + G).

items.hr: Moram li presjedati? — Ne, vlak je izravan.
items.en: Do I have to change trains? — No, the train is direct.
items.note: izravan = direct; also izravni vlak / izravna linija.

activities.title: Transport workout
activities.intro: Words first, then the instrumental of means, then full timetable questions — exactly the ladder from flashcard to ticket window.

questions.prompt: 'Platform' in Croatian is:
questions.explanation: peron = platform. polazak = departure, presjedanje = transfer, prtljaga = luggage.

questions.prompt: Vlak ___ dvadeset minuta.
questions.explanation: kasniti = to be late: Vlak kasni dvadeset minuta. kašnjenje is the noun (delay), kasno the adverb (late).

questions.prompt: Idem na posao ___ . (by tram)
questions.explanation: Means of transport → bare instrumental: tramvajem. No preposition — 'u tramvaj' would mean 'into the tram'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Putujem ___ .' (by train — vlak)
questions.explanation: vlak + -om → vlakom. Hard final consonant takes -om; soft ones (tramvaj, vozač) take -em.

questions.prompt: The plural of putnik (passenger) is:
questions.explanation: Sibilarization: k → c before -i, so putnik → putnici. (putnike is the accusative plural.)

questions.prompt: Ask from which platform the train to Split leaves: 'S ___ perona polazi vlak za Split?'
questions.explanation: s + genitive: s kojeg(a) perona — 'from which platform'. koji declines like an adjective: kojeg, kojem, kojim.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'I go on foot.'
questions.explanation: Idem pješice. — pješice is an adverb, so no case ending at all. pješke is an equally correct variant.

questions.prompt: Build the announcement: 'The train to Rijeka leaves at nine from platform three.'
questions.answer: Vlak za Rijeku polazi u devet s perona broj tri.
questions.explanation: The full template: vlak za + A (destination), u + time, s perona broj + number. Memorize it whole — you'll hear it verbatim on day 96.

days.title: Present class: pišem, kažem
days.objective: Conjugate the -ati → -jem class (pisati → pišem, kazati → kažem).
days.paretoFocus: This class hides some of the most frequent verbs in the language (pisati, kazati, plakati, slati → šaljem). Unit 30 closes Module 3.
drills: Do Unit 30 of A2.hr — Module 3 complete!
Study Grammar tab: A2 · more present-tense classes.
Conjugate pisati, kazati and slati in full.
Write 5 sentences about writing and sending messages.

items: Conjugate slati (šaljem…) cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review transport vocabulary.

activities.title: The pišem class: -ati verbs that change their skin
activities.intro: A whole family of -ati verbs refuses the -am pattern: their present softens the final stem consonant (s→š, z→ž, k→č, g→ž, h→š) and takes -em endings. Once you know the ja-form, the whole paradigm follows. These are not rare verbs — pisati, kazati and slati are some of the most frequent words in the language.

items.hr: pisati → pišem, pišeš, piše, pišemo, pišete, pišu
items.en: to write → I write…
items.note: s → š. The model verb of the class.

items.hr: kazati → kažem, kažeš, kaže, kažemo, kažete, kažu
items.en: to say → I say…
items.note: z → ž. Kako se kaže…? = How do you say…?

items.hr: slati → šaljem, šalješ, šalje, šaljemo, šaljete, šalju
items.en: to send → I send…
items.note: The wildest change in the class: sl- → šalj-. Just memorize šaljem.

items.en: to cry → I cry…
items.note: k → č.

items.en: to jump → I jump
items.note: k → č again.

items.en: to shout → I shout
items.note: k → č — the third of the k-verbs.

items.en: to lie (tell untruths) → I lie
items.note: g → ž.

items.en: to wave → I wave
items.note: h → š.

items.en: to breathe → I breathe
items.note: s → š, like pisati.

items.en: to show → I show
items.note: A prefix doesn't change the class: pokazati works exactly like kazati.

items.en: to watch → I watch
items.note: Most -ati verbs are still regular -am. This class must be learned verb by verb — the dictionary always gives you the ja-form.

items.hr: Past is tame: pisati → pisao/pisala; slati → slao/slala
items.en: wrote; sent
items.note: The participle comes from the infinitive stem, so no consonant change in the perfekt.

activities.title: Writing and sending — the verbs in action
activities.intro: The class in real sentences about messages, letters and noise. Notice how often these verbs pair with a dative person: pišem TI, šaljem TI, mašem TI.

items.en: I'm writing you a message.
items.note: Dative ti + accusative poruku — the double-object pattern.

items.en: What do you say to that?
items.note: A high-frequency idiom: kazati na + accusative.

items.en: I'm sending you photos from the seaside.

items.hr: Ona uvijek kaže istinu, a on često laže.
items.en: She always tells the truth, and he often lies.
items.note: kaže vs laže — both class members, side by side.

items.hr: Djeca skaču i viču u parku.
items.en: The children are jumping and shouting in the park.
items.note: 3rd plural: skaču, viču — the -u ending of the -em class.

items.en: The baby cries all night.
I'm waving to you from the window!
items.note: mahati komu — dative again.

items.en: I breathe deeply when I'm nervous.
Show me what you're writing!
items.note: Imperative from the present stem: pokažem → pokaži!

items.en: Every week we send letters to grandma.
items.note: baki = dative of baka (recipient).

activities.title: pišem–kažem–šaljem drill
activities.intro: Conjugate the class cold, spot the impostor, then produce full sentences about writing and sending.

questions.prompt: pisati — the ja-form is:
questions.explanation: s → š + -em ending: pišem. 'pisam' is the classic wrong guess from the -am class.

questions.prompt: kazati — on/ona ___:
questions.explanation: z → ž: kažem, kažeš, kaže. kažem is the ja-form, not the on-form.

questions.prompt: slati — the ja-form is:
questions.explanation: slati → šaljem — the biggest stem change in the class. All six forms keep šalj-: šalješ, šalje, šaljemo…

questions.prompt: Give the oni-form of pisati: 'Oni ___ pisma.'
questions.explanation: The -em class ends 3rd plural in -u: pišu, kažu, šalju, plaču.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Beba ___ cijelu noć.' (plakati)
questions.explanation: k → č: plakati → plačem, plačeš, plače. The baby cries all night.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Zašto ___? Reci istinu!' (lagati, ti-form)
questions.explanation: g → ž: lagati → lažem, lažeš. Why are you lying? Tell the truth!

questions.prompt: Which verb does NOT belong to the pišem class?
questions.explanation: gledati is a regular -am verb: gledam. The others soften their consonant: pišem, kažem, mašem.

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'Every week we send letters to grandma.'
questions.explanation: šaljemo (mi-form of slati) + accusative pisma + dative recipient baki.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'I'm sending you a message.'
questions.explanation: Šaljem ti poruku. — šaljem (not 'slam'), dative ti, accusative poruku.

days.title: Week 13 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate dative, conditional and transport talk in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Three modules are done — a spoken checkpoint now catches any drift before the intensive middle stretch of the course.
drills: 15-minute conversation: how you get around + what you'd change about local transport.
Apply corrections from your posted plans-and-wishes text.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 25-30.
Record 60 seconds and listen for clitic-order slips.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences from your text.
Recall 6 dative verbs and the conditional paradigm.

activities.title: Week 13 in ten lines
activities.intro: Everything this week taught, one line each. Say each Croatian line aloud before reading the note — any hesitation marks your weakest day, and that's the exercise set to redo before the conversation below.

items.hr: Kad bih imao vremena, učio bih više.
items.en: If I had time, I would study more.
items.note: Day 91: conditional in both halves of the hypothetical.

items.hr: Kome bi prvo javio? — Svojoj ženi.
items.en: Who would you tell first? — My (own) wife.
items.note: Day 91: javiti + dative; svoj because the subject owns.

items.hr: Mi bismo putovali, vi biste ostali.
items.en: We would travel, you would stay.
items.note: Days 91-92: bismo/biste — the careful forms.

items.en: I would like to speak Croatian fluently.
items.note: Day 92: wishes in the conditional.

items.en: Tomorrow I will look for a new job.
items.note: Day 92: real plans in futur I, ću in second position.

items.hr: Idem na posao tramvajem, a vraćam se pješice.
items.en: I go to work by tram and come back on foot.
items.note: Day 93: instrumental of means; pješice is an adverb.

items.hr: S kojeg perona polazi vlak za Rijeku?
items.en: From which platform does the train to Rijeka leave?
items.note: Day 93: the timetable question — s + genitive, za + accusative.

items.hr: povratna karta, red vožnje, kašnjenje, presjedanje
items.en: return ticket, timetable, delay, transfer
items.note: Day 93: the four station words you can't travel without.

items.hr: pisati → pišem, slati → šaljem, kazati → kažem
items.en: write, send, say
items.note: Day 94: the consonant-softening -em class.

items.en: Show me what you're writing!
items.note: Day 94: the imperative grows out of the present stem: pokažem → pokaži.

activities.title: Week 13 mixed review
activities.intro: All four topics shuffled, the way real speech serves them. No labels — you decide whether each question needs a conditional, an instrumental, a dative or the pišem class.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'by bus'?
questions.explanation: Means of transport → bare instrumental: autobusom.

questions.prompt: slati — the mi-form is:
questions.explanation: slati → šalj- in every present form: šaljem, šalješ, šalje, šaljemo…

questions.prompt: Ja ___ došao, ali nemam vremena.
questions.explanation: ja → bih: Ja bih došao, ali nemam vremena. (I would come, but I have no time.)

questions.prompt: Koliko košta povratna karta ___ Zadar?
questions.explanation: Tickets and trains take za + accusative for the destination: karta za Zadar, vlak za Rijeku.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Rado ___ kupili veći brod.' (mi — kondicional)
questions.explanation: mi → bismo: Rado bismo kupili. (In a full 'kad' sentence it appears in both halves: Kad bismo imali…, kupili bismo.)

questions.prompt: Pomogao bih ___ . (my own brother)
questions.explanation: pomoći komu → dative: svom(u) bratu. svog brata is the accusative/genitive — wrong case for pomoći.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'I would send you a photo.' (poslati, fotografija — male speaker)
questions.explanation: Poslao bih ti fotografiju. — participle first, then the chain bih (aux) → ti (dative), and fotografiju in the accusative.

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'If I got a job in Zagreb, I would travel by train every day.'
questions.answer: Kad bih dobio posao u Zagrebu, putovao bih vlakom svaki dan.
questions.explanation: The whole week in one sentence: conditional in both clauses, u Zagrebu (locative), vlakom (instrumental of means).

activities.title: Kako se voziš — i što bi promijenio?
activities.intro: The week 13 spoken checkpoint: your commute in the instrumental, then city fixes in the conditional. Read it aloud, then improvise your real answers to her questions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hajde, mali test prije večere: kako ideš na posao?
lines.en: Come on, a little test before dinner: how do you get to work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Idem tramvajem do kolodvora, a onda pješice deset minuta.
lines.en: I go by tram to the station, and then ten minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično — tramvajem, instrumental! A što bi promijenio u našem gradu?
lines.en: Excellent — tramvajem, instrumental! And what would you change in our city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad bih ja bio gradonačelnik, tramvaji nikad ne bi kasnili.
lines.en: If I were the mayor, the trams would never be late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha! A što biste još napravili, gospodine gradonačelniče?
lines.en: Ha! And what else would you do, Mr. Mayor?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kupio bih sto novih autobusa i izgradio bih više biciklističkih staza.
lines.en: I would buy a hundred new buses and build more bike paths.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Izgradio bih — bravo! A bi li se ti vozio na posao biciklom?
lines.en: Izgradio bih — bravo! And would YOU ride a bike to work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne bih. Ja bih se vozio službenim autom, naravno.
lines.en: I wouldn't. I would ride in an official car, of course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tipičan političar! Sve bi promijenio, ali autom bi se vozio.
lines.en: A typical politician! You'd change everything, but you'd still drive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Šalim se. Najviše volim ići pješice — s tobom.
lines.en: I'm joking. What I like most is going on foot — with you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar spas. Za tu rečenicu dobivaš kavu.
lines.en: Nice save. For that sentence you get a coffee.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A kad bih dobio i kolač, bio bih najsretniji čovjek u gradu.
lines.en: And if I also got a cake, I would be the happiest man in town.

days.title: Tickets, timetables, announcements
days.objective: Understand spoken travel information: platforms, delays, announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Catching essential info from short public announcements is a named A2 can-do — and the difference between catching and missing your bus.
drills: Do Unit 31 of A2.hr.
Listen to the unit dialogues twice without reading, then once with the text.
Extract from each: destination, time, platform/stop, price.
Role-play buying a ticket with one complication (delay or transfer).

items: Say the numbers 100-1000 in tens, fast.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 5 announcement words (polazak, kašnjenje…).

activities.title: Announcement decoder
activities.intro: Station announcements recycle the same dozen phrases. Learn the skeleton and you'll always catch the four essentials: destination, time, platform, delay. Read each line aloud in your best loudspeaker voice — today's exercise plays them to you as audio.

items.hr: Vlak za Split polazi u devet i trideset s perona broj tri.
items.en: The train to Split departs at 9:30 from platform three.
items.note: The master template: destination (za + A), time (u…), platform (s perona broj…).

items.hr: Vlak iz Osijeka stiže na peron broj jedan.
items.en: The train from Osijek is arriving at platform one.
items.note: Arrivals: iz + G (where from), na + A (which platform).

items.hr: Vlak za Rijeku kasni petnaest minuta.
items.en: The train to Rijeka is fifteen minutes late.
items.note: kasni + the time in the accusative. The word you dread.

items.en: because of works on the track
items.note: zbog + genitive — the all-purpose railway excuse. pruga → na pruzi (g → z, sibilarization in the locative!).

items.en: because of traffic congestion
items.note: The bus-station version of the same excuse.

items.hr: Molimo putnike da ne ostavljaju prtljagu bez nadzora.
items.en: We ask passengers not to leave luggage unattended.
items.note: Molimo + accusative + da… — the security announcement on loop at every station.

items.hr: Autobus za Zadar polazi sa stajališta broj pet.
items.en: The bus to Zadar departs from stop number five.
items.note: s becomes sa before st-: sa stajališta.

items.en: Next stop: Ban Jelačić Square.
items.note: The tram announcement you'll hear daily in Zagreb.

items.hr: Putnici za Split presjedaju u Ogulinu.
items.en: Passengers for Split change trains in Ogulin.
items.note: presjedati + u + locative of the transfer town.

items.hr: Vlak je upravo stigao. / Vlak je otkazan.
items.en: The train has just arrived. / The train is cancelled.
items.note: otkazan — the only word worse than kašnjenje.

items.hr: u devet i deset — u pola deset — u petnaest do deset
items.note: pola deset = HALF OF THE TENTH HOUR = 9:30, not 10:30. The number-one listening trap.

activities.title: At the ticket window — with a complication
activities.intro: Your complete ticket-buying script, plot twists included: a transfer AND a delay. Read both roles aloud twice — the second time, cover the English. This is the role-play; perform it with your partner tonight.

items.hr: Dobar dan, jednu povratnu kartu za Rijeku, molim.
items.en: Good afternoon, one return ticket to Rijeka, please.
items.note: jednu povratnu kartu — the whole phrase in the accusative.

items.hr: Za kada? — Za danas, sljedeći vlak.
items.en: For when? — For today, the next train.

items.hr: Prvi ili drugi razred? — Drugi razred, molim.
items.en: First or second class? — Second class, please.
That's eighteen euros.
items.note: After 5+ the noun goes genitive plural: osamnaest eura.

items.hr: Kada polazi vlak? — U deset i dvadeset s perona broj dva.
items.en: When does the train leave? — At 10:20 from platform two.

items.hr: Moram li presjedati? — Da, presjedate u Ogulinu.
items.en: Do I have to change? — Yes, you change in Ogulin.
items.note: Complication no. 1: the transfer. presjedate — polite vi-form.

items.hr: Koliko traje putovanje? — Oko četiri sata.
items.en: How long does the trip take? — About four hours.
items.note: trajati = to last; četiri sata — the paucal form after 2-4.

items.en: Unfortunately the train is twenty minutes late.
items.note: Complication no. 2: the delay. nažalost = unfortunately.

items.hr: Mogu li platiti karticom? — Naravno. Izvolite kartu.
items.en: Can I pay by card? — Of course. Here's your ticket.
items.note: platiti karticom — the instrumental of means strikes again.

items.en: Thank you. Goodbye! — Have a good trip!
items.note: Sretan put — what they'll say as they hand you the ticket.

activities.title: Listening: catch the essentials
activities.intro: Three real announcements, played as audio only. From each, extract the essentials — destination, time, platform, delay — then finish with the phrases and the pola-deset trap on paper.

questions.prompt: Listen to the announcement: where is the train going, and when?
questions.explanation: Vlak za Split polazi u devet i trideset s perona broj tri. — za Split (destination), u devet i trideset (9:30).
questions.audioText: Vlak za Split polazi u devet i trideset s perona broj tri.

questions.prompt: Listen: which platform, and how late is the train?
questions.explanation: Vlak za Rijeku polazi s perona broj četiri. Zbog gužve u prometu vlak kasni deset minuta. — platform four, ten minutes' delay.
questions.audioText: Vlak za Rijeku polazi s perona broj četiri. Zbog gužve u prometu vlak kasni deset minuta.

questions.prompt: 'zbog radova na pruzi' means:
options: because of works on the track
questions.answer: because of works on the track
questions.explanation: zbog + G (because of) + radovi (works) + na pruzi (on the track). The standard railway excuse.

questions.prompt: Listen and type the delay as one word (the number you hear).
questions.explanation: Zbog radova na pruzi vlak za Osijek kasni dvadeset minuta. — twenty minutes late.
questions.audioText: Zbog radova na pruzi vlak za Osijek kasni dvadeset minuta.

questions.prompt: 'Putnici za Split presjedaju u Ogulinu.' — what must Split passengers do?
options: Buy a new ticket in Ogulin
questions.explanation: presjedati = to change trains; u Ogulinu = in Ogulin (locative). Your complication word from the ticket window.

questions.prompt: Ask the clerk whether you have to change trains.
questions.explanation: Moram li presjedati? — modal + li in second position. Moram li presjesti? (perfective) is just as good.

questions.prompt: Rebuild the announcement: 'The train to Zadar departs at eight from platform one.'
questions.answer: Vlak za Zadar polazi u osam s perona broj jedan.
questions.explanation: Destination → time → platform: the fixed skeleton of every departure announcement.

questions.prompt: The announcement says the bus leaves 'u pola deset'. That is:
questions.explanation: pola deset = half of the TENTH hour = 9:30. Croatian counts the hour being filled, not the hour just passed — the classic trap that costs learners their bus.

days.title: Staying in touch: zvati/nazvati, slati/poslati
days.objective: Talk about calls, messages and mail with the right aspect every time.
days.paretoFocus: Communication verbs come in aspect pairs you'll use daily — nazvati me / zvati me is exactly the distinction that makes your Croatian precise.
drills: Do Unit 32 of A2.hr.
Drill the pairs: zvati/nazvati, slati/poslati, javljati se/javiti se, odgovarati/odgovoriti.
Narrate yesterday's communications: who called, who wrote, who replied.
Combine with clitics: Poslao sam ti je jučer. (I sent it to you yesterday.)

items: Give both aspects of 4 communication verbs.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Call me tomorrow!' and 'He was calling me all day.'

activities.title: Aspect pairs for staying in touch
activities.intro: Communication verbs come in pairs: the imperfective for the process or habit, the perfective for one completed contact. English hides this ('he called' vs 'he was calling'); Croatian builds it into the verb. Learn each pair as one unit — imperfective / perfective.

items.en: to be calling / to call (once)
items.note: Zvao me cijeli dan. (kept calling) vs Nazvao me u osam. (one call)

items.en: to be sending / to send (off)
items.note: Yesterday's šaljem class + the prefix po- = the perfective.

items.hr: javljati se / javiti se — javljam se / javim se
items.en: to keep in touch / to get in touch (once)
items.note: Both take the DATIVE: javiti se komu. Also: to answer the phone.

items.en: to be answering / to answer (once)
items.note: odgovoriti na poruku = to answer a message (na + A).

items.en: to be writing / to write (and finish)
to be getting / to get, receive
items.note: Dobivam puno mailova. (constantly) vs Dobio sam tvoj mail. (that one)

items.en: I called you three times!
items.note: Repeated attempts → imperfective zvati.

items.en: I'll call you tonight.
items.note: One future call → perfective nazvati.

items.en: Get in touch with me when you arrive!
items.note: Perfective imperative for one concrete check-in. Clitics: mi (dat) + se.

items.hr: Ona mi se javlja svaki tjedan.
items.en: She gets in touch with me every week.
items.note: Habit → imperfective javljati se. Clitic order: mi → se.

items.en: I answered your message.
items.note: One completed answer → odgovoriti.

items.hr: Nazovi me sutra! / Zvao me je cijeli dan.
items.en: Call me tomorrow! / He was calling me all day.
items.note: The day's flagship pair: one call → nazovi; all-day process → zvao.

activities.title: Yesterday's communications — narration with clitics
activities.intro: Now narrate a whole day of calls and messages in the perfekt, stacking the clitics as you go: aux → dative → accusative. Every line here is a template for describing YOUR yesterday.

items.en: Yesterday mom called me.
items.note: nazvala — one completed call, feminine participle.

items.en: I sent it (the message) to you yesterday.
items.note: The chain: sam (aux) → ti (dative) → je (accusative, = poruku). This is the plan's showcase sentence.

items.en: I was writing emails all morning.
items.note: Process, duration → imperfective pisati.

items.en: I wrote only two messages.
items.note: Completed count → perfective napisati.

items.hr: Javio sam se šefu prije podne.
items.en: I got in touch with the boss before noon.
items.note: javiti se + dative: šefu.

items.en: You didn't answer my message!
items.note: nisi is a stressed word, so the clitic mi tucks in right after it.

items.en: I answered you right away!
items.note: Her reply — feminine odgovorila.

items.hr: Dobio sam tvoju razglednicu iz Splita.
items.en: I got your postcard from Split.

items.hr: Zvala sam te, ali nisi se javio.
items.en: I called you, but you didn't pick up.
items.note: javiti se = also 'to answer the phone'. Negative: nisi se javio.

items.hr: Nazvat ću je sutra i sve ću joj objasniti.
items.en: I'll call her tomorrow and explain everything to her.
items.note: je = her (accusative, after nazvati); joj = to her (dative, after objasniti).

activities.title: Right aspect, right clitic
activities.intro: Pick the right member of each pair, then stack the clitics. The test of today: 'Call me tomorrow!' and 'He was calling me all day' must come out with different verbs.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to call' as ONE completed call?
questions.explanation: nazvati is the perfective: one completed call. zvati is the ongoing/repeated calling.

questions.prompt: ___ me sutra! (Call me tomorrow!)
questions.explanation: One concrete future call → perfective imperative: Nazovi me sutra! (Zovi me would suggest 'keep calling me'.)

questions.prompt: 'He was calling me all day.' = ?
questions.explanation: All-day process → imperfective zvati → zvao. Nazvao (perfective) can't stretch over a whole day of ringing.

questions.prompt: Complete with two clitics: 'Javi ___ ___ kad stigneš!' (get in touch with ME)
questions.explanation: javiti se + dative: Javi mi se! The chain: dative (mi) before se.

questions.prompt: Give the ja-form of poslati (perfective 'send').
questions.explanation: poslati conjugates like slati: šaljem → pošaljem. Poslat ću ti = futur; pošaljem = e.g. 'čim pošaljem' (as soon as I send).

questions.prompt: Which aspect fits? 'Cijelo jutro sam ___ mailove.'
questions.explanation: cijelo jutro = duration → imperfective pisati → pisao. Napisao sam would need a completed result, not a stretch of time.

questions.prompt: Poslao sam ___ jučer. (it — the message — to you)
questions.explanation: Dative before accusative: Poslao sam ti je. (sam → ti → je). 'je ti' reverses the chain and is impossible.

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'I called you, but you didn't pick up.' (female caller)
questions.answer: Zvala sam te, ali nisi se javio.
questions.explanation: First clause: sam → te after the participle. Second clause: nisi is stressed, so se follows it — nisi se javio.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'I answered her immediately.' (male speaker, odgovoriti)
questions.explanation: Odgovorio sam joj odmah. — one completed answer → perfective; joj = dative 'to her'.

days.title: Directions in the city
days.objective: Give and follow multi-step directions.
days.paretoFocus: Directions chain imperatives, accusatives and locatives into the most practical navigation skill there is.
drills: Do Unit 33 of A2.hr.
Learn: skrenite lijevo/desno, idite ravno, preko puta, iza ugla, do semafora.
Explain the route from your home to the nearest shop, step by step.
Follow a spoken 4-step route from the unit and sketch it.

items: Give 3-step directions from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review communication verb pairs.

activities.title: Direction phrases — the navigation toolkit
activities.intro: Directions chain vi-imperatives with landmarks in the right case: do + G (up to), pokraj + G (past), preko puta + G (opposite), u + A (into a street). Learn these twelve and you can both ask for and give any route in a Croatian city.

items.hr: Oprostite, kako mogu doći do kolodvora?
items.en: Excuse me, how can I get to the station?
items.note: The question that starts it all: doći do + genitive.

items.en: Turn left. / Turn right.
items.note: skrenuti → skrenem → imperative skreni/skrenite (the krenuti family).

items.en: Go straight ahead.
Go straight to the traffic lights.
items.note: do + genitive: do semafora, do trga, do mosta.

items.en: At the crossroads turn right.
items.note: na križanju — locative for where you are when you turn.

items.en: across the street from the bank
items.note: preko puta + genitive = opposite. Kafić je preko puta banke.

items.en: around the corner
items.note: Pekara je odmah iza ugla. — iza + genitive.

items.hr: prva ulica lijevo, druga ulica desno
items.en: first street on the left, second street on the right
Cross the street.
items.note: prijeći → prijeđem → imperative prijeđi/prijeđite.

items.en: Go past the post office.
items.note: pokraj + genitive: pokraj pošte, pokraj parka.

items.en: on the other side of the street

items.hr: Je li daleko? — Ne, pet minuta pješice.
items.en: Is it far? — No, five minutes on foot.
items.note: Yesterday's pješice pays off immediately.

activities.title: A route, step by step: from home to the shop
activities.intro: A complete route — the exact monologue to rebuild afterwards about your own street. Each step is one imperative + one landmark; say them in order, then close your eyes and repeat the chain.

items.hr: Izađite iz zgrade i skrenite lijevo.
items.en: Exit the building and turn left.
items.note: Step 1: iz + genitive (out of the building).

items.en: Go straight to the traffic lights.
items.note: Step 2.

items.en: At the lights, cross the street.
items.note: Step 3: na semaforu = locative.

items.en: Go past the pharmacy and the bakery.
items.note: Step 4: pokraj + genitive, twice — ljekarne, pekare.

items.en: Take the first street on the right.
items.note: Turning INTO a street = u + accusative: u prvu ulicu.

items.hr: Trgovina je odmah iza ugla, preko puta parka.
items.en: The shop is just around the corner, opposite the park.
items.note: Two location phrases stacked: iza ugla + preko puta parka.

items.en: All together: five minutes on foot.

items.hr: Ako vidite crkvu, otišli ste predaleko!
items.en: If you see the church, you've gone too far!
items.note: The sentence every local ends directions with. predaleko = too far.

activities.title: Navigate or get lost
activities.intro: Imperatives, the right case after each preposition, and finally following a multi-step route. If you can pass this, you can find any pekara in Croatia.

questions.prompt: 'Turn right' (polite/plural) is:
questions.explanation: Vi-imperative of skrenuti: skrenite. The infinitive or present form is not a command.

questions.prompt: Idite ravno ___ semafora.
questions.explanation: do + genitive = up to: do semafora. It marks the endpoint of the straight stretch.

questions.prompt: Kafić je preko puta ___ . (opposite the bank)
questions.explanation: preko puta + genitive: preko puta banke. banku is the accusative, banci the dative/locative.

questions.prompt: Complete the command: '___ ulicu!' (Cross — vi-form of prijeći)
questions.explanation: prijeći → prijeđem → Prijeđite ulicu! Watch the đ and the ije.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Skrenite u ___ ulicu desno.' (first)
questions.explanation: Turning into a street = u + accusative, and prva agrees with ulicu: u prvu ulicu.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'The bakery is just around the corner.'
questions.explanation: iza + genitive: iza ugla. odmah (right/just) makes it sound native.

questions.prompt: Chain two steps: 'At the lights cross the street and go past the post office.'
questions.answer: Na semaforu prijeđite ulicu i prođite pokraj pošte.
questions.explanation: na semaforu (locative — where), prijeđite + accusative (ulicu), prođite pokraj + genitive (pošte).

questions.prompt: Follow the route: 'Prođite pokraj pošte, prijeđite ulicu i trgovina je preko puta parka.' What do you do right after the post office?
questions.explanation: The chain is: past the post office → prijeđite ulicu (cross the street) → the shop is opposite the park.

days.title: Clitics under pressure
days.objective: Keep clitic order intact in questions and fast exchanges.
days.paretoFocus: 'Možeš li mi ga poslati?' — questions with li plus two pronoun clitics are where order breaks down in real speech. Drilling it now makes it unbreakable.
drills: Do Unit 34 of A2.hr.
Build 8 questions stacking li + dative + accusative clitics.
Answer each at speed, replacing nouns with pronouns.
Review Grammar tab: A2 · clitic order and self-test the chain.

items: Say 'Can you send it to me?' instantly.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the clitic chain (li → aux → dat → acc → se → je).

activities.title: The clitic chain under pressure
activities.intro: The template never changes: li → auxiliary → DATIVE → ACCUSATIVE/GENITIVE → se — and the auxiliary je always goes dead last. Today you drill it at question speed: 'Možeš li mi ga poslati?' has to come out as one breath, not four decisions.

items.hr: Možeš li mi ga poslati? — Mogu, poslat ću ti ga odmah.
items.en: Can you send it to me? — I can, I'll send it to you right away.
items.note: Question: li → mi (dat) → ga (acc). Answer: ću → ti → ga. Same chain, new clitics.

items.hr: Jesi li mu je dao? — Jesam, dao sam mu je jučer.
items.en: Did you give it (f.) to him? — I did, I gave it to him yesterday.
items.note: li → mu (dat) → je (acc). je here = 'it/her', not the auxiliary.

items.hr: Hoćeš li nam ih pokazati? — Pokazat ću vam ih večeras.
items.en: Will you show them to us? — I'll show them to you tonight.
items.note: nam/vam (dat) before ih (acc) — always.

items.hr: Možete li mi je opisati? — Mogu, opisat ću vam je.
items.en: Can you describe her to me? — I can, I'll describe her to you.

items.hr: Sjećaš li ga se? — Sjećam ga se jako dobro.
items.en: Do you remember him? — I remember him very well.
items.note: sjećati se takes the GENITIVE: ga sits in the ACC/GEN slot, se after it.

items.en: She gave it to me.
items.note: The auxiliary je comes after everything: mi → ga → je. Memorize this sentence as the template.

items.en: He laughed at him.
items.note: With se, the auxiliary je disappears entirely: not 'se je'.

items.hr: Kupit ću joj ga za rođendan.
items.en: I'll buy it for her for her birthday.
items.note: ću → joj (dat) → ga (acc).

items.en: Give them to me!
items.note: Imperatives host the clitics immediately after: daj → mi → ih.

items.en: Why didn't you send it to me?
items.note: After zašto, the clitics mi ga grab second position — before the stressed nisi.

items.en: I'll introduce her to you tomorrow.
items.note: ću → ti → je: aux, dative, accusative — the chain one more time.

items.hr: Vidjet ćemo se sutra. — Javit ću ti se kad krenem.
items.en: See you tomorrow. — I'll get in touch when I set off.
items.note: se closes the chain: ćemo se; ću ti se.

activities.title: Clitic order gauntlet
activities.intro: Nine rounds, mostly under time pressure in your head: build the question, fire back the answer, never let a clitic jump the queue.

questions.prompt: The clitic chain, in order, is:
options: li → aux → dative → accusative → se → je
li → dative → aux → accusative → je → se
aux → li → accusative → dative → se → je
je → aux → dative → accusative → se → li
questions.answer: li → aux → dative → accusative → se → je
questions.explanation: li first, then the auxiliaries (sam, ću, bih…), then dative, then accusative/genitive, then se — and the auxiliary je last of all.

questions.prompt: 'Can you send it to me?' = ?
questions.explanation: li right after the verb, then dative (mi), then accusative (ga): Možeš li mi ga poslati?

questions.prompt: 'She gave it to me.' = ?
questions.explanation: mi (dat) → ga (acc) → je (aux, always last). And je can never stand first.

questions.prompt: Complete with two clitics: 'Poslat ću ___ ___ sutra.' (to you + it, masculine)
questions.explanation: ću (aux) → ti (dative) → ga (accusative): Poslat ću ti ga sutra.

questions.prompt: Build the question: 'Did you give it (f.) to him?'
questions.explanation: Jesi hosts the chain: li → mu (dat) → je (acc). Here je is 'it/her', sitting in the accusative slot.

questions.prompt: Build the question: 'Will you show them to us?'
questions.explanation: li → nam (dative) → ih (accusative), then the infinitive. The chain survives any verb you hang it on.

questions.prompt: Answer at speed: 'Možeš li mi je poslati?' — 'Mogu, poslat ću ___ ___ odmah.'
questions.explanation: Flip the persons: mi → ti, keep je (it, f.): Poslat ću ti je odmah.

questions.prompt: Build the reproach: 'Why didn't you send it to me?'
questions.explanation: zašto takes first position, so the clitics mi ga claim second — BEFORE the stressed negative nisi.

questions.prompt: On ___ nasmijao. (He laughed at him.)
questions.explanation: Dative (mu) before se — and with se the auxiliary je vanishes: On mu se nasmijao, never 'se je'.

days.title: Day 100: verbs of motion & aspect
days.objective: Use ići and its perfective family (otići, doći, stići, krenuti) accurately.
days.paretoFocus: One hundred days in, and the motion-verb family is the perfect emblem of your progress: one imperfective base, a fleet of precise perfectives.
drills: Do Unit 35 of A2.hr.
Map the family: ići (go) → otići (leave), doći (arrive), stići (make it), krenuti (set off).
Narrate a door-to-door trip using at least 4 of them.
Celebrate day 100 by listing 5 things you can say now that you couldn't on day 1.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Conjugate doći and krenuti in the present.
Re-tell your door-to-door trip cold.

activities.title: The ići family: one base, four precise perfectives
activities.intro: Day 100! The perfect emblem of how far you've come: the imperfective base ići (go) plus a fleet of perfectives that pin down the exact moment — krenuti (set off), otići (leave), doći (arrive/come), stići (make it). Learn the present stems and participles, then narrate the trip below.

items.en: to go (be going) → I go; went
items.note: The imperfective base: the journey itself, the process.

items.en: to set off → I set off; set off
items.note: The -nuti class: krenem, kreneš… krenu.

items.en: to leave, go away → I leave; left
items.note: Present odem, but the participle keeps the t: otišao.

items.en: to come, arrive → I come; came
items.note: The đ family: dođem, dođeš… dođu.

items.en: to make it, arrive (in time) → I arrive; arrived
items.note: stignem — the -nem present; stigao/stigla in the perfekt.

items.hr: Krenuo sam od kuće u osam.
items.en: I set off from home at eight.
items.note: The trip starts: krenuti + od + genitive.

items.en: I went by tram to the station.
items.note: The journey in progress → imperfective ići + instrumental.

items.en: The train left on time.
items.note: na vrijeme = on time — rarer than kašnjenje, treasure it.

items.hr: Stigao sam u Zadar točno u podne.
items.en: I arrived in Zadar exactly at noon.
items.note: stići = you made it; u + accusative for the destination.

items.en: I left work early.
items.note: otići s + genitive: s posla.

items.hr: Doći ću po tebe u pet.
items.en: I'll come pick you up at five.
items.note: doći po + accusative = come to fetch someone.

items.hr: Dok sam išao kući, nazvala me žena.
items.en: While I was walking home, my wife called me.
items.note: Imperfective in progress (išao), interrupted by a perfective event (nazvala) — the aspect system in one sentence.

activities.title: Motion-verb precision test
activities.intro: Conjugations first, then choosing the exact family member. If you can narrate a door-to-door trip without reaching for 'ići' five times, you've earned your day-100 badge.

questions.prompt: doći — the ja-form is:
questions.explanation: doći → dođem, dođeš, dođe… dođu. The đ appears in every present form.

questions.prompt: krenuti — the ja-form is:
questions.explanation: -nuti → -nem: krenem, kreneš… krenu. (krećem belongs to kretati, the imperfective cousin.)

questions.prompt: The masculine past participle of stići is:
questions.explanation: stići → stigao, stigla, stiglo. Stigao sam! = I made it!

questions.prompt: ___ sam od kuće u sedam. (set off)
questions.explanation: The start of a trip → krenuti: Krenuo sam od kuće u sedam. išao = was going, došao = came, stigao = arrived.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ sam u Split u podne.' (arrived / made it — stići, male speaker)
questions.explanation: Stigao sam u Split u podne. — stići for the arrival moment, u + accusative for the destination.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ana je ___ s posla ranije.' (left — otići)
questions.explanation: otići → feminine participle otišla: Ana je otišla s posla ranije.

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'While I was walking home, my wife called me.'
questions.answer: Dok sam išao kući, nazvala me žena.
questions.explanation: Imperfective backdrop (dok sam išao) + perfective event (nazvala me). kući with no preposition = homeward.

questions.prompt: Translate: 'I'll come pick you up at five.' (doći po…)
questions.answer: Doći ću po tebe u pet.
accepted: Doći ću po tebe u pet
doći ću po tebe u pet.
questions.explanation: Doći ću po tebe u pet. — doći po + accusative (the stressed tebe after a preposition), -ći verbs keep the full infinitive before ću.

activities.title: Day 100: door to door — and a toast
activities.intro: One hundred days. The exam: narrate yesterday's trip using the whole ići family, with your favourite examiner listening. Then celebrate — you've earned every line of this.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znaš li koji je danas dan? Stoti dan mog hrvatskog!
lines.en: Do you know what day it is? Day one hundred of my Croatian!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stoti?! Onda zaslužuješ svečani ispit. Ispričaj mi jučerašnje putovanje, od vrata do vrata.
lines.en: One hundred?! Then you deserve a ceremonial exam. Tell me about yesterday's trip, door to door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može. Krenuo sam od kuće u osam i išao sam tramvajem do glavnog kolodvora.
lines.en: Sure. I set off from home at eight and went by tram to the main station.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro. I onda?
lines.en: Good. And then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vlak je krenuo na vrijeme, ali je stigao u Zadar s malim kašnjenjem.
lines.en: The train left on time, but arrived in Zadar with a small delay.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: S malim kašnjenjem — kao pravi hrvatski vlak. Nastavi!
lines.en: With a small delay — like a true Croatian train. Continue!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Došao sam u hotel oko dva, a poslije sam otišao na plažu.
lines.en: I got to the hotel around two, and afterwards I went off to the beach.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Savršeno! Krenuo, išao, stigao, došao, otišao — cijela obitelj glagola!
lines.en: Perfect! Set off, went, arrived, came, left — the whole verb family!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prije sto dana znao sam reći samo 'dobar dan' i 'pivo, molim'.
lines.en: A hundred days ago I only knew how to say 'good day' and 'a beer, please'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sada mi pričaš o redu vožnje i kašnjenjima. Ponosna sam na tebe.
lines.en: And now you're telling me about timetables and delays. I'm proud of you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad bih morao birati ponovno, opet bih učio hrvatski — zbog tebe.
lines.en: If I had to choose again, I would learn Croatian again — because of you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ajme, sad ćeš me rasplakati! Idemo proslaviti — častim večerom.
lines.en: Oh no, you're going to make me cry! Let's go celebrate — dinner's on me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može! A sljedećih sto dana? Idemo dalje!
lines.en: Deal! And the next hundred days? Onward!

days.title: Write it: a complaint or inquiry
days.objective: Write a short formal message about a transport or service problem.
days.paretoFocus: A polite complaint mixes formal register, conditionals and precise facts — real-world writing you'll genuinely need, and a perfect correction target.
drills: Do Unit 36 of A2.hr.
Write 8-10 sentences: a late bus, a lost package or a wrong charge (what, when, what you request).
Use vi-forms and one polite conditional (Molim Vas… / Biste li mogli…).
Read it to your partner and fix what they flag.

items: Read the message aloud in a formal tone.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review directions vocabulary.

activities.title: Formal message toolkit: the polite complaint
activities.intro: Today you write real formal Croatian: a complaint or inquiry about a late bus, a lost package or a wrong charge. The register is built from a small set of fixed blocks — a formal opener, obraćam Vam se, precise facts in the past tense, one polite conditional, and the standard sign-off. Note that in formal writing Vi, Vam and Vaš are capitalized throughout.

items.en: Dear Sir, / Dear Madam,
items.note: The formal opener. If you don't know the person, plain 'Poštovani,' works for any addressee.

items.hr: Obraćam Vam se u vezi s kašnjenjem autobusa.
items.en: I am writing to you regarding the bus delay.
items.note: obraćati se + dative (Vam); u vezi s + instrumental. The standard first sentence of any formal message.

items.hr: Dana 3. srpnja putovao sam autobusom iz Zagreba u Split.
items.en: On 3 July I travelled by bus from Zagreb to Split.
items.note: Dana + date sets the facts. Instrumental of means: autobusom.

items.hr: Autobus je kasnio više od sat vremena.
items.en: The bus was more than an hour late.
items.note: kasniti = to be late (imperfective — it describes the whole delay). više od = more than.

items.hr: Nitko nam nije dao nikakvu informaciju.
items.en: Nobody gave us any information.
items.note: Croatian stacks negatives: nitko… nije… nikakvu — all three are obligatory.

items.en: My package still has not arrived.
items.note: pošiljka = package, shipment. još nije = still hasn't.

items.en: You charged me the wrong amount.
items.note: naplatiti + dative of the person (mi); iznos = amount.

items.en: I request a refund.
items.note: povrat novca = refund (literally 'return of money'). Molim Vas + the thing you want.

items.hr: Biste li mi mogli poslati potvrdu e-poštom?
items.en: Could you send me the confirmation by e-mail?
items.note: The polite conditional question: Biste li + mogli + infinitive. Your best formal request tool.

items.hr: Molim Vas da mi odgovorite u roku od sedam dana.
items.en: Please reply to me within seven days.
items.note: u roku od + genitive = within (a deadline).

items.en: I am sending you the receipt in the attachment.
items.note: u privitku = attached (in an e-mail). Fronting Račun pushes the clitic Vam into second position.

items.en: Thank you in advance for your reply.
items.note: zahvaljivati na + locative (na odgovoru). A near-obligatory closing line.

items.en: Yours sincerely,
items.note: The only formal sign-off you need. Then your full name underneath.

activities.title: Complain politely, in writing
activities.intro: Assemble a formal complaint block by block: opener, facts, request, sign-off — with the register and the conditional under control.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct opener for a formal complaint?
questions.explanation: Poštovani, (or Poštovani gospodine / Poštovana gospođo) is the standard formal opener. The others are for friends and family.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal first sentence: Obraćam ______ se u vezi s kašnjenjem vlaka.
questions.explanation: obraćati se + dative: Obraćam Vam se… In formal writing the pronoun Vi/Vam is capitalized.

questions.prompt: Which request is polite enough for a formal message?
options: Biste li mi mogli vratiti novac?
questions.answer: Biste li mi mogli vratiti novac?
questions.explanation: The conditional question Biste li mogli…? is the formal, polite request. The imperatives sound like demands.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'The bus was more than an hour late.'
questions.answer: Autobus je kasnio više od sat vremena
questions.explanation: kasniti = to be late; više od sat vremena = more than an hour. The auxiliary je sits in second position.

questions.prompt: Ask for your money back: Molim Vas ______ novca.
questions.explanation: povrat novca = a refund, literally 'return of money': Molim Vas povrat novca.

questions.prompt: Complete the double negation: Nitko mi ______ odgovorio na poruku.
questions.explanation: With nitko the verb must also be negative: Nitko mi nije odgovorio. (Nobody answered me.)

questions.prompt: Complete the closing line: Unaprijed ______ na odgovoru.
questions.explanation: Unaprijed zahvaljujem na odgovoru = Thank you in advance for your reply — zahvaljivati na + locative.

questions.prompt: How do you sign off a formal complaint?
questions.explanation: S poštovanjem (with respect) is the formal sign-off. The others are casual goodbyes between friends.

days.title: Week 14 speaking day (+ Unit 37)
days.objective: Talk travel and transport live, then bank one lighter unit.
days.paretoFocus: Speaking weeks keep pace with study weeks. A travel conversation with your tutor — or your Croatian spouse over coffee, which counts just the same — cements the whole module.
drills: 15-minute conversation: journeys, transport gripes and travel plans.
Do Unit 37 of A2.hr (a lighter one — you've earned it).
Retell your best travel story with clean aspect choices.
Note 5 corrections and add unknown words to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your 5 corrected sentences.
Recall the motion-verb family (ići → otići, doći, stići, krenuti).

activities.title: Travel talk: stories, gripes and plans
activities.intro: Speaking day. Today's phrases carry a whole travel conversation: asking how a trip went, complaining about the journey, telling what happened, and planning the next one. Every line recycles this module's motion verbs and past-tense aspect — say each one aloud before your conversation.

items.en: How did the trip go?
items.note: proći = to go/pass (of events). Putovanje is neuter, so: je prošlo.

items.hr: Prošlo je odlično, bez ikakvih problema.
items.en: It went great, without any problems.
items.note: bez + genitive; ikakvih = any at all.

items.en: The crowds/traffic were terrible.
items.note: gužva covers both traffic jams and crowds — a national conversation topic.

items.en: We had to change (buses) in Karlovac.
items.note: presjedati = to transfer/change vehicles. morati + infinitive.

items.hr: Krenuli smo rano, a stigli smo tek navečer.
items.en: We set off early, but arrived only in the evening.
items.note: tek = only, not until — a tiny word that carries the complaint.

items.hr: Autobus mi je pobjegao za pet minuta.
items.en: I missed the bus by five minutes.
items.note: Colloquial and very common: the bus 'ran away from me' (dative mi).

items.en: The ride took three hours.
items.note: trajati = to last. Vožnja je trajala… — your duration phrase.

items.en: We were lucky with the weather.
items.note: imati sreće — sreća goes into the genitive in this fixed phrase.

items.en: Something funny happened to us.
items.note: dogoditi se + dative — the story-opener that makes people listen.

items.en: Next time we're going by train.
items.note: Instrumental of means: vlakom, autobusom, autom.

items.hr: Planiramo put na more u kolovozu.
items.en: We're planning a trip to the seaside in August.
items.note: put na more = trip to the coast; u + locative for months (u kolovozu).

items.hr: Volim putovati vlakom jer mogu čitati.
items.en: I like travelling by train because I can read.
items.note: A ready-made opinion line for the conversation.

activities.title: Warm-up before the conversation
activities.intro: Prime the phrases you'll need live: trip questions, travel gripes and past-tense aspect choices.

questions.prompt: Ask how the trip went: Kako ______ putovanje?
questions.explanation: putovanje is neuter, so the participle is prošlo: Kako je prošlo putovanje?

questions.prompt: Complete the gripe: Krenuli smo rano, a ______ smo tek navečer.
questions.explanation: stići → stigli (m. pl. participle): we set off early but arrived only in the evening.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'We had to change in Karlovac.'
questions.explanation: morati + infinitive; the auxiliary smo takes second position: Morali smo presjedati…

questions.prompt: 'Next time we're going by train.' — Sljedeći put idemo ______.
questions.explanation: Means of transport takes the bare instrumental: idemo vlakom, autobusom, autom.

questions.prompt: Complete: Vožnja je ______ tri sata. (to last, past)
questions.explanation: trajati = to last; vožnja is feminine, so: Vožnja je trajala tri sata.

questions.prompt: Open a funny story: Dogodilo ______ se nešto smiješno.
questions.explanation: dogoditi se + dative: Dogodilo nam se nešto smiješno. (Something funny happened to us.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I missed the bus by five minutes.'
questions.answer: Autobus mi je pobjegao za pet minuta
questions.explanation: The clitics mi je sit in second position, dative before the auxiliary je: Autobus mi je pobjegao…

questions.prompt: Pick the right aspect: Dok smo ______ na autobus, počela je kiša.
questions.explanation: Dok (while) introduces background, so the imperfective čekali is needed; the event (počela je kiša) is perfective.

activities.title: Uz kavu: priče s putovanja
activities.intro: Over coffee, you and your wife relive a chaotic trip to Dubrovnik. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — the whole conversation runs on today's travel phrases and clean past-tense aspect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ajme, sjećaš se našeg puta u Dubrovnik? Kakva avantura!
lines.en: Oh my, do you remember our trip to Dubrovnik? What an adventure!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sjećam se! Krenuli smo rano ujutro, a stigli smo tek navečer.
lines.en: I remember! We set off early in the morning and arrived only in the evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Autobus je kasnio, a onda smo morali presjedati u Splitu.
lines.en: The bus was late, and then we had to change in Split.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, i autobus mi je pobjegao za pet minuta. Bio sam jako ljut.
lines.en: Yes, and I missed the bus by five minutes. I was really angry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A onda je počela kiša. Čekali smo na stanici bez kišobrana!
lines.en: And then the rain started. We waited at the station without an umbrella!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ali imali smo sreće — jedan vozač nas je povezao do centra.
lines.en: But we were lucky — a driver gave us a ride to the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno! I dogodilo nam se nešto smiješno: mislio je da smo turisti iz Njemačke.
lines.en: Right! And something funny happened to us: he thought we were tourists from Germany.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja jesam stranac, ali ti si mu odgovorila na čistom hrvatskom!
lines.en: I really am a foreigner, but you answered him in flawless Croatian!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha! A kako ćemo putovati sljedeći put? Opet autobusom?
lines.en: Ha! And how will we travel next time? By bus again?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne, hvala. Sljedeći put idemo vlakom ili autom.
lines.en: No, thanks. Next time we're going by train or by car.

days.title: Narrate a journey
days.objective: Tell a complete past-tense travel story with controlled aspect.
days.paretoFocus: Narration is the A2 speaking task: sequencing events (perfective) against background (imperfective) with najprije/zatim/potom/kasnije as signposts.
drills: Do Unit 38 of A2.hr.
Outline a real trip: 6 events, 2 background details.
Tell it aloud with najprije, zatim, potom, na kraju.
Tell it again 20% faster without notes.

items: Re-tell the story in 45 seconds.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Justify the aspect of 3 verbs you used.

activities.title: Signposts for a story: najprije, zatim, potom
activities.intro: Narration is the A2 speaking task, and it runs on two rails: sequencing words that mark each event, and the aspect rule — perfective verbs for the events, imperfective for the background. Learn the signposts, then watch them steer a real journey story.

items.en: first (of all)
items.note: Opens the narration: Najprije smo… (First we…)

items.en: then / after that
items.note: Interchangeable sequencers — vary them so the story doesn't sound mechanical.

items.en: after that
items.note: nakon + genitive: nakon toga, nakon ručka, nakon puta.

items.en: in the meantime
items.note: Introduces background — typically with an imperfective verb.

items.en: suddenly
items.note: The plot-twist word. What follows is almost always perfective.

items.en: in the end / finally
items.note: Closes the story: Na kraju smo ipak stigli. (ipak = after all.)

items.hr: Najprije smo se odvezli na kolodvor.
items.en: First we drove to the station.
items.note: odvesti se = to drive (somewhere). Event = perfective.

items.hr: Zatim smo kupili karte i našli svoja mjesta.
items.en: Then we bought the tickets and found our seats.
items.note: A chain of perfective events: kupili, našli. svoja mjesta — svoj for one's own.

items.hr: Dok smo putovali, gledao sam krajolik.
items.en: While we were travelling, I watched the landscape.
items.note: dok + imperfective = background. krajolik = landscape.

items.hr: Odjednom je vlak stao usred polja.
items.en: Suddenly the train stopped in the middle of a field.
items.note: stati → stao (perfective). usred + genitive = in the middle of.

items.hr: Potom smo čekali pola sata bez informacija.
items.en: Then we waited half an hour without any information.
items.note: čekati stays imperfective — the waiting is a stretch of time, not a point.

items.hr: Na kraju smo ipak stigli na vrijeme.
items.en: In the end we arrived on time after all.
items.note: na vrijeme = on time. The perfective stigli closes the story.

activities.title: Sequence and aspect
activities.intro: Build the journey story yourself: signpost each step and pick perfective for events, imperfective for background.

questions.prompt: Start the story: ______ smo kupili karte, zatim smo ušli u vlak.
questions.explanation: najprije = first (of all) — the standard opener of a narration, paired with zatim/potom.

questions.prompt: Pick the right aspect for the background: Dok smo ______, gledao sam krajolik.
questions.explanation: dok (while) frames background, which takes the imperfective: Dok smo putovali… The prefixed forms are perfective.

questions.prompt: Close the story with the perfective: Na kraju smo ipak ______ na vrijeme. (stići)
questions.explanation: stići → stigli (m. pl.): Na kraju smo ipak stigli na vrijeme. (In the end we arrived on time after all.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Then we waited half an hour without any information.'
questions.answer: Zatim smo čekali pola sata bez informacija
questions.explanation: Signpost first, auxiliary smo in second position, then the imperfective čekali for the stretch of waiting.

questions.prompt: A completed one-time event: Jučer sam ______ kartu online.
questions.explanation: One finished purchase = perfective kupiti → kupio. kupovao would suggest repeated or ongoing buying.

questions.prompt: Add background with the imperfective: Dok sam čekao vlak, ______ sam kavu. (piti)
questions.explanation: Background drinking alongside background waiting: Dok sam čekao vlak, pio sam kavu.

questions.prompt: What does 'u međuvremenu' mean?
questions.explanation: u međuvremenu = in the meantime — it introduces what was going on in the background.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Suddenly the train stopped in the middle of a field.'
questions.answer: Odjednom je vlak stao usred polja
questions.explanation: odjednom + perfective (stao) marks the sudden event; usred + genitive = in the middle of (polja).

activities.title: Ispričaj mi cijelo putovanje
activities.intro: Your wife plays coach: she asks you to narrate your journey out loud from start to finish. Read your lines ('Me') with the signposts in place — she checks your aspect as you go.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hajde, ispričaj mi svoje putovanje od početka. Vježbaj naglas!
lines.en: Come on, tell me your journey from the beginning. Practise out loud!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može. Najprije smo se rano ujutro odvezli na kolodvor.
lines.en: Okay. First, early in the morning, we drove to the station.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobro. Što je bilo zatim?
lines.en: Good. What happened then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zatim smo kupili karte i našli svoja mjesta u vlaku.
lines.en: Then we bought the tickets and found our seats on the train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A dok ste putovali? Dodaj malo pozadine.
lines.en: And while you were travelling? Add some background.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dok smo putovali, gledao sam krajolik i pio kavu.
lines.en: While we were travelling, I watched the landscape and drank coffee.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično — tu ide nesvršeni glagol. Nastavi!
lines.en: Excellent — that's where the imperfective verb goes. Continue!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Odjednom je vlak stao usred polja. Potom smo čekali pola sata.
lines.en: Suddenly the train stopped in the middle of a field. Then we waited half an hour.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I kako je sve završilo?
lines.en: And how did it all end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na kraju smo ipak stigli na vrijeme. Nitko nije vjerovao!
lines.en: In the end we arrived on time after all. Nobody could believe it!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo! Sad ispričaj sve još jednom, bez pauza i bez bilježaka.
lines.en: Bravo! Now tell the whole thing once more, without pauses and without notes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ajme… dobro. Najprije smo se odvezli na kolodvor…
lines.en: Oh boy… okay. First we drove to the station…

days.title: Listening: short recorded messages
days.objective: Extract the essentials from voicemails and short announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding short recorded messages is a named A2 can-do and tomorrow's mock-listening warm-up — train the exact skill, not around it.
drills: Do Unit 39 of A2.hr.
Play each unit audio once only and write: who, what, when, where.
Check against the transcript; replay only what you missed.
Shadow the fastest dialogue line by line.

items: Summarise one audio in 2 Croatian sentences.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note your listening weak spot (speed, numbers, names?).

activities.title: Voicemail & announcement decoder
activities.intro: Short recorded messages are a named A2 can-do: a voicemail from the dentist, a station announcement, a shop's opening hours. The trick is not understanding every word — it's catching the four essentials: tko, što, kada, gdje. These are the fixed phrases those recordings are built from.

items.en: voicemail (voice message)
items.note: Ostaviti govornu poruku = to leave a voicemail.

items.en: Leave a message after the beep.
items.note: zvučni signal = the beep. You'll hear this on every Croatian answering machine.

items.hr: Zovem u vezi s vašim terminom.
items.en: I'm calling about your appointment.
items.note: termin = appointment slot. u vezi s + instrumental again — it opens phone messages too.

items.hr: Termin je pomaknut na četvrtak u deset.
items.en: The appointment has been moved to Thursday at ten.
items.note: pomaknut = moved — learn it as a fixed word from real messages; pomaknut na + accusative (the new time).

items.hr: Molim vas, nazovite nas danas do 16 sati.
items.en: Please call us back today by 4 p.m.
items.note: do + genitive = by (a deadline). nazvati = to call (perfective).

items.hr: Ako je hitno, javite se na broj 091…
items.en: If it's urgent, call the number 091…
items.note: hitno = urgent; javiti se na broj = to call a number.

items.hr: Vlak iz Osijeka kasni dvadeset minuta.
items.en: The train from Osijek is running twenty minutes late.
items.note: The classic announcement pattern: vehicle + iz/za + delay.

items.hr: Polazak je s perona broj dva.
items.en: The departure is from platform two.
items.note: polazak = departure; peron = platform. s + genitive (s perona).

items.en: The flight is cancelled due to the weather.
items.note: otkazan = cancelled — another fixed announcement word; zbog + genitive = because of.

items.hr: Trgovina danas radi do dvadeset sati.
items.en: The shop is open today until 8 p.m.
items.note: raditi do = to be open until. Recorded on every store's phone line.

items.en: I will call you tomorrow morning.
items.note: How messages often end — futur I with the clitics ću vas in second position.

items.en: who, what, when, where
items.note: Your listening checklist. One pass of the audio, four facts on paper — that's the whole drill.

activities.title: One pass, four facts
activities.intro: Real listening practice: play each recording once only and catch who, what, when, where — then lock in the message vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Listen to the voicemail once: what is the message?
options: The appointment is moved to Thursday at ten
The appointment is moved to Tuesday at four
The dentist is calling to say hello
questions.answer: The appointment is moved to Thursday at ten
questions.explanation: Vaš termin je pomaknut s utorka na četvrtak u deset sati. = Your appointment has been moved from Tuesday to Thursday at ten. The call-back deadline is today by four.
questions.audioText: Dobar dan, zovem iz zubne ordinacije. Vaš termin je pomaknut s utorka na četvrtak u deset sati. Molim vas, nazovite nas danas do šesnaest sati.

questions.prompt: Listen to the station announcement: what do you learn?
questions.explanation: Vlak za Rijeku polazi s perona broj dva… kasni petnaest minuta. = The train to Rijeka departs from platform two and is running fifteen minutes late because of track works.
questions.audioText: Vlak za Rijeku polazi s perona broj dva. Zbog radova na pruzi vlak kasni petnaest minuta.

questions.prompt: Listen and type the missing word: Trgovina danas radi do ______ sati.
questions.explanation: Trgovina danas radi do dvadeset sati. = The shop is open today until 8 p.m. Numbers are a classic listening weak spot — write them down immediately.
questions.audioText: Dobar dan. Trgovina danas radi do dvadeset sati. Nedjeljom ne radimo.

questions.prompt: What does 'Ostavite poruku nakon zvučnog signala.' mean?
options: Leave a message after the beep.
questions.answer: Leave a message after the beep.
questions.explanation: zvučni signal = the beep; ostaviti poruku = to leave a message. The standard answering-machine line.

questions.prompt: Complete the announcement: Vlak iz Osijeka ______ dvadeset minuta.
questions.explanation: kasniti = to run late: Vlak iz Osijeka kasni dvadeset minuta. (The train from Osijek is twenty minutes late.)

questions.prompt: Complete the airport announcement: Let je ______ zbog vremena.
questions.explanation: otkazan = cancelled — the fixed announcement form: Let je otkazan zbog vremena. (The flight is cancelled due to the weather.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I will call you tomorrow morning.'
questions.explanation: Futur I with the clitic chain in second position: ću (auxiliary) → vas (accusative).

questions.prompt: Which four essentials should you catch from any short recorded message?
questions.explanation: Who, what, when, where — one pass of the audio, four facts. That's the entire listening strategy at A2.

days.title: Mid-phase milestone: Units 1-40 done
days.objective: Finish Unit 40 — the exact halfway point — and verify your progress with a mock listening drill.
days.paretoFocus: Half the e-course, four of eight modules, all core case declensions and the conditional behind you. Measuring now tells you precisely what the second half must fix.
drills: Do Unit 40 of A2.hr — Module 4 complete, Units 1-40 done!
Mock listening drill: 3 unit audios from different modules, one pass each, score your comprehension honestly.
Self-check: declining a full phrase in A, L, D; clitic order; 10 aspect pairs; the conditional.
Write down your 3 weakest areas — they get priority in weeks 15-21.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-listen to the audio you scored worst and close the gap.
Say out loud: halfway done — 40 units to go.

activities.title: Halfway there: weeks 9-15 in ten sentences
activities.intro: Unit 40 done — the exact midpoint of the e-course. Before the mini-mock below, run through the whole phase in ten model sentences: one per big topic, from language adjectives to directions. If you can produce each one cold, the second half starts from solid ground.

items.hr: Govorim engleski, a učim hrvatski jezik.
items.en: I speak English, and I'm learning Croatian.
items.note: Week 9: language adjectives in -ski, lowercase in Croatian (hrvatski, engleski).

items.hr: Volim svoju ženu i svoj posao.
items.en: I love my wife and my job.
items.note: Week 9: svoj — always for the subject's own things, in every gender and case.

items.hr: Čitam zanimljivu knjigu o hrvatskoj povijesti.
items.en: I'm reading an interesting book about Croatian history.
items.note: Week 9: accusative across the whole phrase (zanimljivu knjigu) + o + locative.

items.en: I'll bring it to you tomorrow.
items.note: Week 10: the clitic chain — ću (aux) → ti (dative) → ga (accusative), all in second position.

items.hr: Nađemo se u pet ispred kina?
items.en: Shall we meet at five in front of the cinema?
items.note: Week 10: arrangements with naći se; ispred + genitive.

items.hr: Živimo u malom stanu u mirnom kvartu.
items.en: We live in a small flat in a quiet neighbourhood.
items.note: Week 11: locative agreement across the whole phrase — u malom stanu.

items.hr: Pisao sam poruku pola sata i na kraju sam je napisao.
items.en: I was writing the message for half an hour and finally finished it.
items.note: Weeks 11-12: aspect — pisao (process) vs napisao (completion).

items.en: Could you help me? (polite)
items.note: Week 12: the conditional — bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi — as the politeness engine.

items.hr: Poklonit ću svojoj ženi cvijeće za rođendan.
items.en: I'll give my wife flowers for her birthday.
items.note: Week 13: dative across the phrase (svojoj ženi) + futur I.

items.hr: Skrenite desno kod pekare i idite ravno do trga.
items.en: Turn right at the bakery and go straight to the square.
items.note: Week 14: directions with formal imperatives; kod + genitive, do + genitive.

activities.title: Mid-phase mini-mock: weeks 9-15
activities.intro: Ten questions, one sweep of everything since the phase began: -ski adjectives, svoj, case agreement, clitics, aspect, the conditional and directions. Score yourself honestly — what you miss here gets priority in the coming weeks.

questions.prompt: Ona govori francuski, a njezin muž uči ______ jezik. ('Croatian')
questions.explanation: Language adjectives in -ski are lowercase and agree with jezik (m.): hrvatski jezik. Hrvatska (capitalized) is the country.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right possessive: Marko voli ______ posao. (his own)
questions.explanation: The subject's own things take svoj: Marko voli svoj posao. (njegov would point to someone else's job.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I'll bring it to you tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: The clitic chain in second position: ću (auxiliary) → ti (dative) → ga (accusative), then the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Poznaješ li Ivana? — Da, poznajem ______.
questions.explanation: The accusative clitic ga replaces Ivana: Poznajem ga. (I know him.) mu is dative.

questions.prompt: Živimo ______ stanu. ('in a small flat')
questions.explanation: The locative runs through the whole phrase: u malom stanu — adjective and noun both in the locative.

questions.prompt: Pick the right aspect: Cijelo poslijepodne sam ______ pisma, ali nisam sve napisao.
questions.explanation: A whole afternoon of ongoing activity = imperfective pisao; the (failed) completion is expressed by napisao.

questions.prompt: Make it polite (Vi): ______ li mi mogli pomoći?
questions.explanation: The conditional auxiliary for vi is biste: Biste li mi mogli pomoći? (Could you help me?)

questions.prompt: Complete the dative phrase: Kupio sam poklon ______ ženi. (svoj)
questions.explanation: Dative feminine of svoj: svojoj ženi — Kupio sam poklon svojoj ženi. (I bought a present for my wife.)

questions.prompt: Give a stranger directions politely: '______ desno kod pekare.'
questions.explanation: The vi-imperative for strangers: Skrenite desno kod pekare. (Turn right at the bakery.) Skreni is the ti-form.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'First we bought the tickets and boarded the train.'
questions.answer: Najprije smo kupili karte i ušli u vlak
questions.explanation: Week 15 narration: the signpost najprije opens, the auxiliary smo takes second position, and both events are perfective.

days.title: Odnosi, izgled, budućnost — describing people
days.objective: Describe people's appearance and character in detail.
days.paretoFocus: izgled i karakter vocabulary (visok, plavokos, duhovit, strpljiv) is the raw material for every story that involves a person — which is most stories.
drills: Do Unit 41 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 appearance/character adjectives.
Describe two people you know: 3 sentences of looks, 3 of character.
Mind full adjective agreement in every case you use.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe a family member in 4 sentences.
Review your 3 weak areas from the milestone.

activities.title: Izgled i karakter: describing people
activities.intro: Appearance and character vocabulary is the raw material of every story that involves a person — which is most stories. Learn the adjectives in pairs of opposites, and mind the agreement: every adjective matches its noun in gender, number and case.

items.en: tall / short
items.note: Moja žena je visoka; njezin djed je nizak. Feminine adds -a: visoka, niska.

items.en: slim / plump
items.note: The polite pair for build. Feminine: vitka, punašna.

items.en: blond / dark-haired
items.note: kosa = hair, built into the adjective: plavokosa djevojka = a blonde girl.

items.hr: Ima kratku smeđu kosu i zelene oči.
items.en: He/She has short brown hair and green eyes.
items.note: imati + accusative: kratku smeđu kosu (f.), zelene oči (pl.).

items.hr: Nosi naočale i uvijek se smiješi.
items.en: He/She wears glasses and is always smiling.
items.note: nositi naočale = to wear glasses; smiješiti se = to smile.

items.en: witty, funny
items.note: The compliment Croatians value most. Ona je jako duhovita.

items.en: patient / impatient
