Note corrections; queue new words in the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences.
Recall: dajem/prodajem + dio declension.

activities.title: Questions that open family stories
activities.intro: Speaking day. Tonight you interview your wife about the names in her family — and these questions carry the whole conversation. They recycle po + locative, possessives, svoj and the perfekt, but they feel like curiosity, not grammar. Say each one aloud before you use it live.

items.en: Who were you named after? (to a woman)
items.note: To a man: Po kome si dobio ime?

items.en: Do you celebrate your name day? How?
items.note: slaviti = to celebrate; li right after the verb makes the question.

items.en: Where does your surname come from?
items.note: potjecati → potječe = to originate, stem from.

items.hr: Kako su se zvali tvoji baka i djed?
items.en: What were your grandma and grandpa called?
items.note: zvati se in the perfekt: su se zvali.

items.en: Who were your godparents?
items.note: Dative ti = 'to you'; kumovi — day 131's word in the plural.

items.hr: Postoji li neka priča o tvom imenu?
items.en: Is there a story behind your name?
items.note: postojati → postoji = to exist; priča o + locative.

items.hr: Je li to ime često u tvojoj obitelji?
items.en: Is that name common in your family?
items.note: Je li…? — the standard yes/no question with je.

items.hr: Koje je najstarije ime u obitelji?
items.en: What is the oldest name in the family?
items.note: najstarije — superlative, neuter to agree with ime.

items.hr: Ispričaj mi nešto o svojoj baki.
items.en: Tell me something about your grandma.
items.note: svojoj — her own grandma, because she is the subject; ispričati = to tell (a story).

items.en: Really? I didn't know that!
items.note: Your best listening reaction — use it often tonight.

items.hr: Čekaj, ponovi — tko je kome dao ime?
items.en: Wait, say that again — who gave whom the name?
items.note: Clarify without switching to English: ponovi = repeat (imperative).

items.hr: Kod nas nema imendana — to mi je novo.
items.en: Where I'm from there are no name days — that's new to me.
items.note: nema + genitive; to mi je novo = that's new to me.

activities.title: Warm-up: interview your wife
activities.intro: Two listening passes and six quick checks — prime your ears and your questions before the real conversation about names and family history.

questions.prompt: Listen to your wife's answer: when and where does she celebrate her name day?
options: In July, at her mum's place
questions.answer: In July, at her mum's place
questions.explanation: Moj imendan je u srpnju… slavimo ga kod moje mame — her name day is in July and the whole family comes to her mum's for lunch.
questions.audioText: Moj imendan je u srpnju, na blagdan svete Ane. Uvijek ga slavimo kod moje mame — dođe cijela obitelj na ručak i mama peče kolače.

questions.prompt: Listen to her talk about her family: what does her grandma's surname mean?
questions.explanation: Prezime moje bake bilo je Kovačić — to znači 'kovačev sin': her great-grandfather was a blacksmith (kovač).
questions.audioText: Moje prezime potječe iz Dalmacije. Prezime moje bake bilo je Kovačić — to znači 'kovačev sin', jer je njezin pradjed bio kovač.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: Odakle ______ tvoje prezime? (potjecati)
questions.explanation: potjecati → potječe: Odakle potječe tvoje prezime? — Where does your surname come from?

questions.prompt: You're asking your wife. Pick the correct form: 'Who were you named after?'
questions.explanation: To a woman the participle is feminine (dobila), and 'named after' is po + locative (po kome).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Tell me something about your grandma.'
questions.answer: Ispričaj mi nešto o svojoj baki
questions.explanation: Imperative first, clitic mi second, then o + locative with svoj: o svojoj baki.

questions.prompt: React to a surprise: Stvarno? To nisam ______! (znati, male speaker)
questions.explanation: znati → znao (m. sg.): To nisam znao! — I didn't know that!

questions.prompt: 'Kod nas nema imendana.' — Which case does nema take?
questions.explanation: nema + genitive for absence: nema imendana, nema kruha, nema problema.

questions.prompt: Ask for a repeat: Čekaj, ______ — nisam razumio. (repeat, imperative to ti)
questions.explanation: ponoviti → ponovi! — your clarification tool that keeps the conversation in Croatian.

activities.title: Večernji razgovor: imena u tvojoj obitelji
activities.intro: Tonight you hold the microphone: you interview your wife about the names in her family. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — they are exactly today's questions and reactions, deployed live.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Večeras te ja intervjuiram. Po kome si dobila ime?
lines.en: Tonight I'm interviewing you. Who were you named after?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Po baki! Ona se zvala isto, a i njezina mama.
lines.en: After my grandma! She had the same name, and so did her mum.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Stvarno? To nisam znao. Znači, tri generacije nose isto ime?
lines.en: Really? I didn't know that. So three generations carry the same name?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da, kod nas se imena nasljeđuju. A slavimo i imendan, naravno.
lines.en: Yes, in our family names are inherited. And we celebrate the name day too, of course.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad ti je imendan? Kod nas toga nema, pa uvijek zaboravim.
lines.en: When is your name day? We don't have that back home, so I always forget.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U srpnju. Mama uvijek peče kolače i dođe cijela obitelj.
lines.en: In July. Mum always bakes cakes and the whole family comes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A odakle potječe tvoje prezime? Zvuči jako staro.
lines.en: And where does your surname come from? It sounds very old.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Iz Dalmacije. Znači 'kovačev sin' — pradjed je bio kovač.
lines.en: From Dalmatia. It means 'the smith's son' — my great-grandfather was a blacksmith.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Čekaj, ponovi — tko je bio kovač? Tvoj pradjed?
lines.en: Wait, say that again — who was a blacksmith? Your great-grandfather?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da, pradjed. Imao je malu radionicu pokraj mora.
lines.en: Yes, my great-grandfather. He had a small workshop by the sea.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Krasna priča. U tvojoj obitelji svako ime ima svoju priču.
lines.en: A wonderful story. In your family every name has its own story.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zato i kažemo: ime je znak. A sad ti — ispričaj mi nešto o svom djedu!
lines.en: That's why we say: a name is a sign. And now you — tell me something about your grandfather!

days.title: Hrvati u svijetu, svijet u Hrvatskoj
days.objective: Open the final theme: diaspora, migration and cultures meeting.
days.paretoFocus: More Croats live abroad than at home — iseljeništvo, domovina, useljenik are the words behind half of Croatian family stories. Unit 70 closes Module 7.
drills: Do Unit 70 of A2.hr — Module 7 complete, one module to go!
Learn 12 words: iseljeništvo, domovina, useljenik, granica, državljanstvo, običaj…
Tell the migration story of a family you know (yours or your spouse's).
Mark on a mental map: where do Croatian communities live worldwide?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'My family moved from… to… because…'.
Note: 70 units down, 10 to go.

activities.title: Hrvati u svijetu: the diaspora vocabulary
activities.intro: More Croats and their descendants live abroad than in Croatia — which is why iseljeništvo, domovina and korijeni sit behind half of Croatian family stories. Today's words let you tell a migration story: who left, who arrived, why, and what they kept.

items.en: emigration; the diaspora (as a whole)
items.note: hrvatsko iseljeništvo = Croats abroad, collectively.

items.en: diaspora
items.note: The international word works too: živjeti u dijaspori.

items.en: homeland
items.note: stara domovina = the old country — how emigrants call Croatia.

items.en: emigrant / immigrant
items.note: iz- = out (leaving), u- = in (arriving) — the prefixes show the direction.

items.en: border
items.note: prijeći granicu = to cross the border.

items.en: citizenship
items.note: imati dvojno državljanstvo = to have dual citizenship — very common in Croatian families.

items.en: custom, tradition
items.note: čuvati stare običaje = to keep old customs.

items.en: roots
items.note: Tražim svoje korijene. — I'm searching for my roots.

items.en: generation
items.note: druga generacija iseljenika = second-generation emigrants.

items.en: to move (change residence)
items.note: Preselili smo se u Zagreb prije dvije godine.

items.hr: Moja se obitelj odselila zbog posla.
items.en: My family moved away because of work.
items.note: odseliti se = to move away; zbog + genitive = because of.

items.hr: Vratili su se u staru domovinu.
items.en: They returned to the old homeland.
items.note: vratiti se u + accusative — motion back.

items.hr: Hrvatske zajednice žive u Njemačkoj, Americi, Australiji i Čileu.
items.en: Croatian communities live in Germany, America, Australia and Chile.
items.note: zajednica = community; u + locative for countries: u Njemačkoj, u Americi.

activities.title: Tell a migration story
activities.intro: Check the direction prefixes (iz- vs u-), the cases behind zbog and u, and the phrases that carry every family's migration story.

questions.prompt: Someone who has just arrived to live in a new country is a(n) ______.
questions.explanation: u- = in (arriving): useljenik = immigrant. iz- = out (leaving): iseljenik = emigrant.

questions.prompt: Complete: Imam dvojno ______. (citizenship)
questions.explanation: dvojno državljanstvo = dual citizenship — a word every diaspora family knows.

questions.prompt: Complete the auxiliary: Preselili ______ se u Zagreb prije dvije godine.
questions.explanation: Perfekt of preseliti se: Preselili smo se… — smo before se in the clitic chain.

questions.prompt: 'They moved away because of work.' — Odselili su se ______ posla.
questions.explanation: zbog + genitive = because of: zbog posla, zbog rata, zbog ljubavi.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'They returned to the old homeland.'
questions.answer: Vratili su se u staru domovinu
questions.explanation: Clitic order su + se in second position, then u + accusative of direction: u staru domovinu.

questions.prompt: Croatian communities live in Germany: Žive u ______.
questions.explanation: Living in a country = u + locative: u Njemačkoj. (u Njemačku would be motion: to Germany.)

questions.prompt: Complete: Tražim svoje ______. (roots)
questions.explanation: korijeni (roots) in the accusative plural: tražim svoje korijene.

questions.prompt: What does 'čuvati stare običaje' mean?
questions.explanation: čuvati = to keep/guard; običaj = custom. Diaspora families čuvaju stare običaje.

days.title: Impersonal sentences: Kaže se…
days.objective: Use impersonal constructions: kaže se, može se, treba, hladno mi je.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonals are how Croatian talks about 'people in general' and inner states — 'Ovdje se dobro jede', 'Hladno mi je' — indispensable and utterly unlike English.
drills: Do Unit 71 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · impersonal sentences.
Build 6 se-impersonals: Kako se kaže…? Ovdje se ne puši. U Hrvatskoj se puno kava pije.
Build 4 dative-state sentences: Hladno mi je. Dosadno joj je. Žao nam je.

items: Say 'How do you say that in Croatian?' impersonally.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review diaspora vocabulary.

activities.title: Kaže se: the impersonal toolkit
activities.intro: Croatian talks about 'people in general' and inner states without a subject: Kaže se… (one says), Ovdje se ne puši (no smoking), Hladno mi je (I'm cold). These patterns are indispensable and utterly unlike English — collect them today as fixed templates.

items.hr: Kako se kaže 'name day' na hrvatskom?
items.en: How do you say 'name day' in Croatian?
items.note: The most useful sentence in this whole app: se + 3rd singular.

items.en: You say 'imendan'.
items.note: The answering pattern — no subject at all.

items.en: No smoking here.
items.note: se-impersonal: literally 'here it is not smoked'.

items.hr: U Hrvatskoj se pije puno kave.
items.en: In Croatia a lot of coffee is drunk.
items.note: The 'people in general' pattern — no one in particular drinks it, everyone does.

items.en: The food is good here. (One eats well here.)
items.note: Day 134's jesti inside an impersonal.

items.en: You can pay by card.
items.note: može se + infinitive = one can; karticom — instrumental of means.

items.hr: Ne smije se parkirati na pločniku.
items.en: You must not park on the pavement.
items.note: ne smije se = it is not allowed — the language of signs.

items.en: One needs to practise a lot.
items.note: treba + infinitive, no subject: general necessity.

items.en: I am cold.
items.note: State + dative experiencer. Never *Ja sam hladan — that means you're a cold person!

items.en: She is bored.
items.note: joj = to her. Vruće mu je — he is hot.

items.hr: Žao nam je što ne možete doći.
items.en: We are sorry that you can't come.
items.note: žao + dative + je; što introduces the reason.

items.hr: Bilo je vruće, a sad je ugodno.
items.en: It was hot, and now it's pleasant.
items.note: Past of impersonals: neuter singular — bilo je.

items.en: One should have studied more!
items.note: Past of treba: trebalo je — also neuter singular.

activities.title: No subject, no problem
activities.intro: Build se-impersonals and dative-state sentences until they come out automatically — including their neuter past.

questions.prompt: Complete: Kako se ______ 'homeland' na hrvatskom?
questions.explanation: Kako se kaže…? = How do you say…? — the impersonal question you'll use forever. (Answer: domovina.)

questions.prompt: Pick the correct sign: 'No smoking here.'
questions.explanation: se-impersonal in the 3rd singular, with se in second position: Ovdje se ne puši.

questions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence: 'I am cold.'
questions.explanation: Neuter adjective + dative experiencer: Hladno mi je. Ja sam hladan would describe your personality.

questions.prompt: Complete: Dosadno ______ je. (to her)
questions.explanation: Dative clitic joj = to her: Dosadno joj je — she is bored.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'In Croatia a lot of coffee is drunk.'
questions.answer: U Hrvatskoj se pije puno kave
questions.explanation: se sits in second position after the opening phrase U Hrvatskoj; puno + genitive (kave).

questions.prompt: Put 'Vruće je.' into the past.
questions.explanation: Impersonal past always takes the neuter singular participle: Bilo je vruće.

questions.prompt: Complete: Može se ______ karticom. (platiti)
questions.explanation: može se + infinitive: Može se platiti karticom — you can pay by card.

questions.prompt: Complete: Žao ______ je što ne možeš doći. (to us)
questions.explanation: žao + dative: Žao nam je — we are sorry.

questions.prompt: What does 'Trebalo je više učiti!' mean?
questions.explanation: Past of impersonal treba is the neuter trebalo je: Trebalo je više učiti — the classic post-exam sigh.

days.title: Comparing cultures
days.objective: Compare life in Croatia with life in your country, respectfully and richly.
days.paretoFocus: Culture comparison recycles the whole phase — comparatives, impersonals ('U Hrvatskoj se večera kasnije'), -ski adjectives — in the conversation every foreigner in Croatia gets asked daily.
drills: Do Unit 72 of A2.hr.
Write 6 comparison sentences: food, work hours, coffee culture, family life.
Use 2 impersonals and 3 comparatives among them.
Answer the classic: Što ti se najviše sviđa u Hrvatskoj?

items: Give your 'best thing about Croatia' answer cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review impersonal patterns.

activities.title: Comparing two worlds: frames that stay friendly
activities.intro: Every foreigner in Croatia gets asked to compare: food, work, coffee, family. Today's frames recycle the whole phase — comparatives, impersonals, dative experiencers — and end with the diplomatic lines that keep the comparison warm: nije bolje ni gore, samo je drukčije.

items.hr: U Hrvatskoj se večera kasnije nego kod nas.
items.en: In Croatia dinner is eaten later than back home.
items.note: Impersonal + comparative adverb — the day's whole formula in one line.

items.hr: Kod nas se radi duže, ali se manje živi.
items.en: Back home people work longer, but live less.
items.note: kod nas = where I'm from; duže and manje — comparative adverbs.

items.hr: Kava se ovdje pije polako, uz razgovor.
items.en: Coffee here is drunk slowly, over conversation.
items.note: uz + accusative = accompanied by: uz razgovor, uz kolač.

items.en: Life here is more relaxed.
items.note: opušten → opušteniji — regular -iji comparative.

items.en: The food is fresher and tastier.
items.note: svjež → svježiji, ukusan → ukusniji (fleeting a drops).

items.hr: Plaće su manje, ali je život jeftiniji.
items.en: Salaries are smaller, but life is cheaper.
items.note: mali → manji (irregular); jeftin → jeftiniji.

items.hr: Obitelj je ovdje važnija nego karijera.
items.en: Family here is more important than career.
items.note: nego + nominative when comparing two nouns.

items.en: What I like most is the sea.
items.note: sviđa mi se + nominative — the dative experiencer from day 139.

items.en: The hardest thing for me is learning the cases.
items.note: najteže + dative + infinitive — an honest, charming answer.

items.hr: I jedno i drugo ima svoje prednosti.
items.en: Both have their advantages.
items.note: prednost (f., i-declension) = advantage. The diplomatic answer.

items.hr: Nije bolje ni gore — samo je drukčije.
items.en: It's not better or worse — just different.
items.note: Your culture-comparison lifeline: bolje/gore are the irregular adverb comparatives.

items.hr: Što ti se najviše sviđa u Hrvatskoj?
items.en: What do you like most about Croatia?
items.note: The classic question every foreigner gets daily — have your answer ready, cold.

activities.title: Compare without offending
activities.intro: Comparatives, impersonals and the dative experiencer — the grammar of the conversation you'll have at every Croatian table.

questions.prompt: Complete: U Hrvatskoj se večera kasnije ______ kod nas.
questions.explanation: Before a phrase like kod nas, comparison uses nego: kasnije nego kod nas.

questions.prompt: Complete the comparative: Život je ovdje ______. (opušten)
questions.explanation: Regular -iji comparative: opušten → opušteniji — more relaxed.

questions.prompt: Pick the comparative of jeftin:
questions.explanation: jeftin takes the regular -iji: jeftiniji; superlative najjeftiniji.

questions.prompt: 'Najviše mi se sviđa more.' — Who does the liking?
options: I do — mi is the dative experiencer
questions.answer: I do — mi is the dative experiencer
questions.explanation: sviđati se puts the liker in the dative (mi) and what is liked in the nominative (more).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Coffee here is drunk slowly.'
questions.explanation: The clitic se takes second position right after Kava: Kava se ovdje pije polako.

questions.prompt: Complete: Plaće su ______, ali je život jeftiniji. (mali, comparative)
questions.explanation: mali has the irregular comparative manji; feminine plural: plaće su manje.

questions.prompt: Pick the diplomatic answer: 'It's not better or worse — just different.'
options: Nije bolje ni gore — samo je drukčije.
Nije dobro ni loše — sve je isto.
Bolje je i gore u isto vrijeme.
questions.answer: Nije bolje ni gore — samo je drukčije.
questions.explanation: bolje/gore are the irregular adverb comparatives of dobro/loše; drukčije = different(ly).

questions.prompt: The classic question: Što ti se najviše ______ u Hrvatskoj?
questions.explanation: Što ti se najviše sviđa u Hrvatskoj? — have your answer ready before dinner tonight.

activities.title: Dva svijeta za jednim stolom
activities.intro: Over dinner, your wife asks the classic question — and you compare your two worlds using today's frames: impersonals, comparatives and the diplomatic finish. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reci mi iskreno — što ti se najviše sviđa u Hrvatskoj?
lines.en: Tell me honestly — what do you like most about Croatia?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Najviše mi se sviđa more. I kava — ovdje se kava pije polako, uz razgovor.
lines.en: What I like most is the sea. And coffee — here coffee is drunk slowly, over conversation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kod vas? Kako se pije kava u tvojoj zemlji?
lines.en: And back home? How is coffee drunk in your country?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kod nas se sve radi brže. Ljudi kupe kavu i piju je u autu.
lines.en: Back home everything is done faster. People buy coffee and drink it in the car.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To mi zvuči stresno. A posao?
lines.en: That sounds stressful to me. And work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kod nas se radi duže, ali mislim da je život ovdje opušteniji.
lines.en: Back home people work longer, but I think life here is more relaxed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Plaće su ovdje manje, znaš.
lines.en: Salaries are smaller here, you know.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Istina, ali je život jeftiniji, a obitelj je važnija nego karijera.
lines.en: True, but life is cheaper, and family is more important than career.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Postoji li nešto što ti je ovdje teško?
lines.en: Is there anything that's hard for you here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Najteže mi je naučiti padeže! I večera se jede prekasno za mene.
lines.en: The hardest thing for me is learning the cases! And dinner is eaten too late for me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha! Navikni se. Pa što je na kraju bolje — tvoja zemlja ili moja?
lines.en: Ha! Get used to it. So which is better in the end — your country or mine?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nije bolje ni gore — samo je drukčije. I jedno i drugo ima svoje prednosti.
lines.en: It's not better or worse — just different. Both have their advantages.

days.title: Read the news (simple items)
days.objective: Read short news items and extract names, numbers and the gist.
days.paretoFocus: Reading simple news with numbers and names is a named A2 can-do — and HRT's short items are the perfect daily habit to carry into B1.
drills: Do Unit 73 of A2.hr.
Read 3 short HRT news items; for each note: who, what, where, when, how many.
Guess 5 unknown words from context before looking them up.
Retell the most interesting item in 3 Croatian sentences.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-tell one news item cold.
Review culture-comparison sentences.

activities.title: Čitamo vijesti — the news-reading toolkit
activities.intro: Thirteen phrases that unlock short news items: the nouns of headlines, the verbs of reporting, and the who-what-where-when-how-many frame you'll apply to three real HRT items today.

items.en: Have you heard the latest news?
items.note: vijest = a news item, a feminine i-declension noun. The plural vijesti = the news.

items.hr: Naslov članka je često pola priče.
items.en: The headline of an article is often half the story.
items.note: naslov = headline, title; članak = article, with a fleeting a in the genitive: članka.

items.hr: Prema izvještaju, cijene i dalje rastu.
items.en: According to the report, prices keep rising.
items.note: prema + dative: izvještaj → izvještaju. Your source-citing frame for retelling news.

items.en: What happened and where?
items.note: dogoditi se = to happen (perfective); the imperfective is događati se. Question one of your news frame.

items.hr: Gradonačelnik je izjavio da će most biti gotov ove godine.
items.en: The mayor stated that the bridge will be finished this year.
items.note: izjaviti = to state (what officials do in news); ove godine is your temporal genitive from day 120.

items.hr: U Zagrebu je jučer otvoren novi muzej.
items.en: A new museum was opened in Zagreb yesterday.
items.note: otvoren = opened, the passive participle — news Croatian loves this pattern: otvoren je, zatvorena je, najavljen je.

items.hr: Na prosvjedu je bilo oko dvije tisuće ljudi.
items.en: There were around two thousand people at the protest.
items.note: prosvjed = protest; oko + genitive = around, approximately. Numbers are your 'how many' answer.

items.en: The elections will be next month.
items.note: izbori = elections, a plural-only noun. sljedećeg mjeseca — temporal genitive again.

items.en: The government announced new measures.
items.note: vlada = government; najaviti = to announce; mjera = measure. Three words that appear in every second news item.

items.hr: Zbog poplave je zatvorena cesta prema Sisku.
items.en: Because of the flood, the road to Sisak is closed.
items.note: poplava = flood; zbog + genitive = because of. The 'why' of a news item almost always hides behind zbog.

items.hr: Koliko je ljudi bilo tamo? — Oko sto pedeset.
items.en: How many people were there? — Around a hundred and fifty.
items.note: koliko + genitive plural (ljudi), and the verb goes neuter singular: koliko je ljudi bilo.

items.hr: Ne razumijem svaku riječ, ali razumijem bit.
items.en: I don't understand every word, but I understand the gist.
items.note: bit (f.) = the gist, the essence. This sentence IS the A2 reading strategy — say it proudly.

items.hr: Najzanimljivija vijest tjedna: u Splitu je počeo filmski festival.
items.en: The most interesting news of the week: a film festival has started in Split.
items.note: Your retelling frame: superlative + vijest tjedna (genitive!) + one clean sentence. Use it on today's third item.

activities.title: Who, what, where, when, how many
activities.intro: News vocabulary first, then two real-style mini items to mine for names, numbers and the gist — exactly what you'll do with HRT today.

questions.prompt: What does 'vijest' mean?
questions.explanation: vijest = a piece of news. Newspaper is novine, headline is naslov, event is događaj.

questions.prompt: The headline of an article is the ___ of the ___:
questions.explanation: naslov = headline, and the article follows in the genitive with its fleeting a: članak → članka.

questions.prompt: Prema ___, sutra će padati kiša. (izvještaj — put it after prema)
questions.explanation: prema takes the dative: izvještaj → izvještaju. 'According to the report…'

questions.prompt: Read: 'U Rijeci je jučer otvoren novi bazen. Gradonačelnica je rekla da je bazen koštao dva milijuna eura.' — How much did the pool cost?
questions.explanation: dva milijuna eura = two million euros. You also caught the who (gradonačelnica), the where (u Rijeci) and the when (jučer).

questions.prompt: Što se ___ u centru grada? (dogoditi se — perfekt)
questions.explanation: Impersonal 'what happened' takes the neuter participle: Što se dogodilo? The se is already there.

questions.prompt: Na koncertu je bilo oko pet tisuća ___.
questions.explanation: Quantities take the genitive plural: pet tisuća ljudi. And oko = around, also with the genitive.

questions.prompt: Report it: The government announced new measures.
questions.explanation: Subject first, clitic je in second position, then the participle: Vlada je najavila nove mjere.

questions.prompt: Read: 'Zbog radova na cesti autobusi od ponedjeljka neće voziti kroz centar. Radovi će trajati tri tjedna.' — How long will the works last?
questions.explanation: tri tjedna = three weeks (paucal form after tri). Bonus catch: buses stop running through the centre from Monday (od ponedjeljka).

questions.prompt: Zbog ___ je zatvorena škola u selu. (poplava)
questions.explanation: zbog + genitive: poplava → poplave. The 'why' of news items lives in zbog + G.

days.title: Watch the news for gist
days.objective: Follow TV news topic-by-topic using visuals as support.
days.paretoFocus: Getting the gist of TV news where visuals support the commentary is the A2 listening ceiling — hit it now and B1 media becomes a step, not a wall.
drills: Do Unit 74 of A2.hr.
Watch 10 minutes of HRT Dnevnik; write one line per story: topic + one fact.
Rewatch one story and count how much more you catch.
Shadow the anchor for 30 seconds — pure pronunciation gold.

items: Summarise 2 stories in Croatian.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note your news comprehension percentage — you'll re-test at B1.

activities.title: Dnevnik decoded — the language of TV news
activities.intro: Tonight you watch ten minutes of HRT's Dnevnik. These twelve lines are the fixed furniture of every broadcast — know them and the anchor suddenly slows down.

items.en: Dnevnik (the evening news) starts at seven p.m.
items.note: Dnevnik = HRT's main evening news, literally 'the daily'. u + number + sati for clock times.

items.hr: Voditeljica čita vijesti iz zemlje i svijeta.
items.en: The anchor reads the news from home and abroad.
items.note: voditelj / voditeljica = (male/female) anchor, host; iz zemlje i svijeta = the standard 'domestic and world' rubric.

items.en: Dear viewers, good evening.
items.note: gledatelj = viewer. This exact greeting opens the broadcast — your first easy win of the evening.

items.hr: Glavna tema večeras su cijene struje.
items.en: Tonight's main topic is electricity prices.
items.note: glavna tema = main topic; struja = electricity (literally 'current'). večeras = tonight.

items.hr: Naš reporter javlja se uživo iz Osijeka.
items.en: Our reporter is reporting live from Osijek.
items.note: uživo = live; javljati se = to report in, check in — the same verb you use for 'to get in touch'.

items.en: Watch our correspondent's report.
items.note: prilog = a news segment/package (in the kitchen it means 'side dish'!); dopisnik = correspondent.

items.en: The weather forecast is coming up.
items.note: slijedi = 'up next follows…' — the anchor's transition word; vremenska prognoza = weather forecast.

items.hr: Sutra sunčano, uz najvišu temperaturu od trideset stupnjeva.
items.en: Sunny tomorrow, with a high of thirty degrees.
items.note: Forecast Croatian drops verbs: Sutra sunčano. stupanj → genitive plural stupnjeva after trideset.

items.en: In sport: Hajduk won 2:1.
items.note: pobijediti = to win (a match); scores are read dva jedan. The sport block closes every Dnevnik.

items.hr: O tome više u nastavku emisije.
items.en: More on that later in the programme.
items.note: u nastavku = in the continuation, later on; emisija = TV programme, broadcast.

items.hr: Razumijem temu, iako ne razumijem svaku riječ.
items.en: I understand the topic, even though I don't understand every word.
items.note: iako = although, even though. Your gist mantra for tonight — one line per story: topic + one fact.

items.en: The picture helps me understand the commentary.
items.note: pomagati + dative (mi) + infinitive. This is the literal A2 can-do: following TV news where visuals support the commentary.

activities.title: News for gist — listen like it's Dnevnik
activities.intro: Three short bulletins read at natural speed: catch the topic and one fact per item, exactly your job tonight. Plus the studio vocabulary that frames them.

questions.prompt: What is 'vremenska prognoza'?
questions.explanation: vremenska prognoza = weather forecast; vrijeme means both 'time' and 'weather'.

questions.prompt: The reporter is on the scene right now. The broadcast is:
questions.explanation: uživo = live. u nastavku = later in the programme.

questions.prompt: Listen to the news item once: what happened in Zagreb?
options: A new bridge over the Sava was opened
A bridge over the Sava was closed
Traffic was stopped in the whole city
questions.answer: A new bridge over the Sava was opened
questions.explanation: U Zagrebu je danas otvoren novi most preko Save. The mayor says it will ease traffic (olakšati promet) — that's your one fact.
questions.audioText: Dobra večer, poštovani gledatelji. U Zagrebu je danas otvoren novi most preko Save. Gradonačelnik kaže da će most olakšati promet u cijelom gradu.

questions.prompt: Listen to the forecast: what's tomorrow's weather in Dalmatia?
options: Sunny and hot, up to 32 degrees
Sunny in the morning, rain in the afternoon
questions.answer: Sunny and hot, up to 32 degrees
questions.explanation: U Dalmaciji sunčano i vruće, do trideset dva stupnja. The rain (kiša) is expected in the north of the country, in the afternoon.
questions.audioText: A sada vremenska prognoza. Sutra će u Dalmaciji biti sunčano i vruće, do trideset dva stupnja. Na sjeveru zemlje poslijepodne se očekuje kiša.

questions.prompt: Listen to the sport announcement: when does the match start?
questions.explanation: Utakmica počinje u dvadeset sati i četrdeset pet minuta = 20:45, against Italy, broadcast on Channel One.
questions.audioText: U sportu: hrvatska reprezentacija večeras igra protiv Italije. Utakmica počinje u dvadeset sati i četrdeset pet minuta, a prijenos možete gledati na Prvom programu.

questions.prompt: Naš reporter javlja se ___ iz Splita. (live)
questions.explanation: uživo = live — the word that flashes in the corner of the screen during live reports.

questions.prompt: Open the broadcast: Tonight's main topic is electricity prices.
questions.answer: Glavna tema večeras su cijene struje
questions.explanation: The plural subject cijene takes su, and struje is the genitive after cijene — 'prices OF electricity'.

questions.prompt: Sutra će biti do trideset ___. (stupanj)
questions.explanation: Numbers from five up take the genitive plural: stupanj → stupnjeva (with the fleeting a gone and -ev- inserted).

days.title: Write it: a letter abroad
days.objective: Write a warm personal letter to a friend or relative in another country.
days.paretoFocus: The personal letter — greetings, thanks, news, questions, linked with i/ali/jer and najprije/zatim/kasnije — is the A2 writing descriptor made real.
drills: Do Unit 75 of A2.hr.
Write ~130 words: Draga…/Dragi…, your news, one memory, questions for them, warm closing.
Check your connectors: at least i, ali, jer plus two sequencers.
Post it for corrections — this is your last corrected text of the phase.

items: Read the letter aloud.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review letter openings and closings (Draga…, Srdačan pozdrav…).

activities.title: Pismo preko granice — the personal letter
activities.intro: Today you write ~130 warm words to someone abroad — your last corrected text of the phase. Here is the full skeleton: openings with vocatives, news frames, memory lines, questions and closings.

items.en: Dear Grandma, how are you?
items.note: The vocative: baka → bako. Draga for women, Dragi for men — always with a comma, then a new line.

items.hr: Dragi Ivane, javljam ti se iz Zagreba.
items.en: Dear Ivan, I'm writing to you from Zagreb.
items.note: Vocative Ivan → Ivane; javljam ti se = literally 'I'm making contact with you' — the standard letter opener.

items.en: Thank you for the lovely letter.
items.note: hvala na + locative: lijepom pismu. Acknowledge their last letter before your news.

items.hr: Kod nas je sve po starom.
items.en: Everything is the same as always with us.
items.note: po starom = 'as of old' — the set phrase for 'no big changes'. A perfect low-effort news line.

items.hr: Javljam ti se jer imam veliku novost.
items.en: I'm writing because I have big news.
items.note: novost = a piece of news (personal news, unlike vijest which is media news). jer = because — one of your three required connectors.

items.hr: Sjećaš li se kad smo se zadnji put vidjeli?
items.en: Do you remember when we last saw each other?
items.note: sjećati se = to remember; the memory paragraph starts here. zadnji put = the last time.

items.hr: Najprije smo se preselili, zatim sam našao posao, a kasnije smo kupili auto.
items.en: First we moved, then I found a job, and later we bought a car.
items.note: najprije → zatim → kasnije: the sequencer chain your letter must show. One sentence, a whole year of news.

items.hr: Kako su svi kod tebe? Piši mi uskoro!
items.en: How is everyone at your end? Write to me soon!
items.note: Questions for them are an A2 letter requirement — ask at least two. piši = imperative of pisati.

items.en: Say hello to everyone at home.
items.note: pozdraviti in the imperative + accusative sve. The pre-closing line of every Croatian family letter.

items.hr: Jedva čekam da se vidimo ovog ljeta.
items.en: I can't wait for us to see each other this summer.
items.note: jedva čekam = I can hardly wait; ovog ljeta — the temporal genitive one more time.

items.en: Warm greetings from Zagreb,
items.note: srdačan = cordial, warm — the closing register between formal (S poštovanjem) and intimate (Voli te…).

items.en: Your grandson Marko loves you. (= Love, your grandson Marko)
items.note: The intimate closing: Voli te + who you are to them. For friends: Grli te (hugs you), Ljubi te (kisses you).

items.hr: P. S. Šaljem ti i nekoliko fotografija.
items.en: P.S. I'm also sending you a few photos.
items.note: slati → šaljem (the -jem class strikes again); nekoliko + genitive plural: fotografija.

activities.title: Build the letter, line by line
activities.intro: Vocatives, the hvala-na frame, sequencers and closings — every question here is a brick for the 130 words you're about to write.

questions.prompt: You're writing to your grandmother. The letter opens:
questions.explanation: Feminine Draga + the vocative bako. The nominative baka stays outside the letter.

questions.prompt: Which closing is warm but not intimate — right for an aunt abroad?
questions.explanation: Srdačan pozdrav = warm greetings. S poštovanjem is for officials; Doviđenja is spoken, not written.

questions.prompt: Dragi ___, kako si? (Ivan — vocative)
questions.explanation: Masculine names in -n take the vocative -e: Ivan → Ivane. (Marko stays Marko.)

questions.prompt: Hvala ti na ___ pismu. (lijep — agree with pismu)
questions.explanation: hvala na + locative, and the adjective follows the neuter noun: lijepom pismu.

questions.prompt: Give the reason for writing: I'm writing to you because I have big news.
questions.answer: Javljam ti se jer imam veliku novost
questions.explanation: Verb-first, then the clitics in order: ti (dative) before se. jer introduces the reason — connector number three.

questions.prompt: ___ smo se preselili, zatim sam našao posao.
questions.explanation: The chain runs najprije → zatim → kasnije/na kraju. Only najprije can open it.

questions.prompt: Jedva ___ da se vidimo ovog ljeta. (čekati, ja)
questions.explanation: jedva čekam da… = I can't wait to… — learn it as one chunk; it makes any letter warmer.

questions.prompt: Pozdravi ___ kod kuće. (svi — accusative)
questions.explanation: pozdraviti takes the accusative: svi → sve. 'Greet everyone at home.'

questions.prompt: Which closing fits a letter to your grandmother?
questions.explanation: Family gets the intimate closing: Voli te + tvoj unuk. The Vaš/gospodine options are formal register — wrong warmth entirely.

days.title: Week 20 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate the world-and-Croatia theme in a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: With 75 units behind you, today's conversation should feel qualitatively different from week 9's — measure that difference and mine what still creaks.
drills: 20-minute conversation: Croatia vs the world — food, customs, news of the week.
Apply corrections from your posted letter.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 70-75.
List the 5 grammar points you still hesitate on — next week's sweeps will target them.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences.
Recall impersonals + one culture comparison.

activities.title: Kod nas i kod vas — comparing worlds
activities.intro: Tonight's 20-minute conversation is Croatia vs the world: food, customs, this week's news. These ten frames let you compare fairly, disagree politely and say what you miss.

items.hr: Kod nas se ručak jede kasnije nego u Hrvatskoj.
items.en: Where I'm from, lunch is eaten later than in Croatia.
items.note: kod nas = 'at ours' — where I'm from / at our place. The impersonal se makes it a general custom, not one person's habit.

items.hr: Za razliku od Hrvatske, kod nas trgovine rade nedjeljom.
items.en: Unlike Croatia, back home the shops are open on Sundays.
items.note: za razliku od + genitive = unlike, in contrast to. nedjeljom = on Sundays (instrumental of time).

items.hr: U Hrvatskoj je obiteljski ručak jako važan.
items.en: In Croatia the family lunch is very important.
items.note: obiteljski = family (adjective from obitelj). Your observation frame: U Hrvatskoj je X važan/važna/važno.

items.hr: Navike su različite, ali ljudi su svugdje slični.
items.en: Habits are different, but people are similar everywhere.
items.note: navika = habit; svugdje = everywhere. The diplomatic summary of any culture comparison.

items.hr: Čini mi se da je život ovdje opušteniji.
items.en: It seems to me that life here is more relaxed.
items.note: čini mi se da = it seems to me that — softer than mislim da. opušten → opušteniji, a regular comparative.

items.hr: S jedne strane volim grad, a s druge strane mi nedostaje priroda.
items.en: On the one hand I love the city, and on the other I miss nature.
items.note: s jedne strane… s druge strane… — the two-sided frame that makes your opinions sound B1 already.

items.hr: Najviše mi nedostaje hrana iz moje zemlje.
items.en: What I miss most is the food from my country.
items.note: nedostajati works backwards: the missed thing is the subject, the misser goes in the dative (mi).

items.hr: Vijest tjedna kod nas je bila poskupljenje goriva.
items.en: The news of the week back home was the fuel price increase.
items.note: poskupljenje = a price increase (from skup, expensive); gorivo = fuel. Yesterday's news skills, recycled into conversation.

items.hr: Slažem se s tobom, ali samo djelomično.
items.en: I agree with you, but only partially.
items.note: slagati se s + instrumental; djelomično = partially. Polite disagreement is an A2 superpower.

items.hr: Što se tebi čini, gdje se bolje živi?
items.en: What do you think — where is life better?
items.note: The question to hand the topic back: impersonal se + comparative adverb bolje. Ask it tonight and just listen.

activities.title: Week 20 mixed sweep
activities.intro: Everything week 20 built — news vocabulary, letter frames, impersonals, comparatives and the comparison toolkit — in one pass before tonight's long conversation.

questions.prompt: What does 'za razliku od' mean?
questions.explanation: za razliku od + genitive = unlike. Because of is zbog, thanks to is zahvaljujući, instead of is umjesto.

questions.prompt: Za razliku ___ Hrvatske, kod nas trgovine rade nedjeljom. (preposition)
questions.explanation: The chunk is fixed: za razliku od + genitive (Hrvatske).

questions.prompt: 'U Hrvatskoj se kava pije polako.' — What does the se-construction express here?
options: A general custom — people drink it that way
Only one person drinks coffee slowly
questions.answer: A general custom — people drink it that way
questions.explanation: The impersonal/passive se generalizes: 'coffee is drunk slowly' = that's how it's done here. Your day-138 grammar, now in conversation.

questions.prompt: Najviše ___ nedostaje more. (ja — dative clitic)
questions.explanation: nedostajati flips the roles: the sea is the subject, and you — the one missing it — take the dative clitic mi.

questions.prompt: Život je ovdje ___ nego u velikom gradu. (opušten)
questions.explanation: Masculine subject život takes the masculine comparative: opušteniji. opuštenije would be the adverb, najopušteniji the superlative.

questions.prompt: Soften your opinion: It seems to me that life here is cheaper.
questions.answer: Čini mi se da je život ovdje jeftiniji
questions.explanation: Čini mi se da… — the clitics mi se follow the verb, and je jumps to second position inside the da-clause.

questions.prompt: Prema ___, sutra počinje kiša. (prognoza)
questions.explanation: prema + dative: prognoza → prognozi. Yesterday's TV vocabulary meets the case system.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: S jedne strane volim grad, a s druge strane mi jako nedostaje priroda.
S jedne strane volim grad, a s druge strane priroda jako nedostaje mi.
S jednoj strani volim grad, a s drugoj strani mi jako nedostaje priroda.
S jedne strane volim grad, a s druge strane ja jako nedostajem prirodu.
questions.answer: S jedne strane volim grad, a s druge strane mi jako nedostaje priroda.
questions.explanation: s + genitive in the set phrase (s jedne strane), the clitic mi in second position of its clause, and priroda as the subject of nedostajati.

activities.title: Speaking night: Croatia vs the world
activities.intro: Your week-20 conversation: customs, coffee, food and the news of the week — with comparisons flying both ways. Act it out, then keep going with your real observations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Večeras pričamo o razlikama. Što ti je u Hrvatskoj još uvijek čudno?
lines.en: Tonight we're talking about differences. What's still strange to you in Croatia?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Iskreno? To što nedjeljom skoro ništa ne radi. U mojoj zemlji trgovine rade cijeli tjedan.
lines.en: Honestly? That almost nothing is open on Sundays. In my country the shops are open all week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zato nedjeljom imamo vremena za ručak s obitelji. A što ti se ovdje više sviđa?
lines.en: That's why on Sundays we have time for lunch with the family. And what do you like better here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kava! Ovdje se kava pije polako, s ljudima. Kod nas se pije u autu, u žurbi.
lines.en: Coffee! Here coffee is drunk slowly, with people. Back home it's drunk in the car, in a hurry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ha! Postaješ pravi Hrvat. A hrana? Budi iskren.
lines.en: Ha! You're becoming a real Croat. And the food? Be honest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tvoja mama kuha bolje nego ijedan restoran u mom gradu. Ali najviše mi nedostaje hrana iz djetinjstva.
lines.en: Your mum cooks better than any restaurant in my city. But what I miss most is the food from my childhood.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je normalno. Svima nedostaje okus doma. A jesi li pratio vijesti ovaj tjedan?
lines.en: That's normal. Everyone misses the taste of home. And did you follow the news this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam. Vijest tjedna kod vas je bila poskupljenje goriva, a kod nas izbori.
lines.en: I did. The news of the week here was the fuel price increase, and back home, the elections.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vidiš kako lijepo uspoređuješ! Prije tri mjeseca to ne bi mogao.
lines.en: See how nicely you're comparing! Three months ago you couldn't have done that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Čini mi se da svakog tjedna razumijem i tebe i Hrvatsku sve bolje.
lines.en: I know. It seems to me that every week I understand both you and Croatia better and better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: S jedne strane si još stranac, a s druge si već pola Hrvata.
lines.en: On the one hand you're still a foreigner, and on the other you're already half Croatian.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda idemo sutra na kavu — polako, bez žurbe, kao pravi Hrvati.
lines.en: Then let's go for coffee tomorrow — slowly, no rush, like real Croats.

days.title: Grammar sweep 1: the full case system
days.objective: Decline any full phrase in any case, on demand.
days.paretoFocus: One timed sweep through the complete declension system — nouns, adjectives, pronouns, svoj, foreign nouns, ime/dijete/dio — turns twelve weeks of pieces into one machine.
drills: Do Unit 76 of A2.hr.
Decline 5 mixed phrases through all 7 cases (include one foreign noun and one stem-extension noun).
Speed round: 20 random case prompts, answer in under 5 seconds each.
Re-study the Grammar tab topic where you were slowest.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline 'moj najbolji prijatelj' through all cases, aloud.
Give the genitive of: taksi, ime, dijete, dio.

activities.title: Grammar sweep 1 — one phrase, seven cases
activities.intro: Twelve weeks of declension in one machine: take 'moj najbolji prijatelj' through every case, then the special troops — taksi, ime, dijete, dio, riječ, kolega. Say every line aloud, fast.

items.en: This is my best friend.
items.note: Nominative — the dictionary form of the whole phrase. Now watch every word change together.

items.en: My best friend isn't here.
items.note: Genitive after nema: moj → mog, najbolji → najboljeg, prijatelj → prijatelja.

items.en: I'm writing to my best friend.
items.note: Dative: -om + -em + -u. The longer forms mome/najboljemu are equally standard.

items.en: I see my (own) best friend.
items.note: Accusative animate = genitive form. And it's svog, not mog — the subject (ja) is the owner.

items.en: Friend, where are you?
items.note: Vocative: prijatelj → prijatelju. Case number five, mostly for calling people.

items.en: We're talking about my best friend.
items.note: Locative — same endings as the dative, always with a preposition (o, u, na, pri).

items.en: I'm travelling with my best friend.
items.note: Instrumental: -im + -im + -em. sa (not s) before svojim — easier to pronounce before s-.

items.en: taxi: genitive taksija, instrumental taksijem
items.note: Foreign nouns in -i insert a -j- before every ending: taksija, taksiju, taksijem. Same with žiri (jury).

items.en: name: genitive imena, plural imena
items.note: Stem extension -en-: ime → imena, imenu… Same family: vrijeme → vremena, rame → ramena.

items.hr: dijete: genitiv djeteta — a množina je djeca!
items.en: child: genitive djeteta — and the plural is djeca!
items.note: Stem extension -et- in the singular; the plural is the collective djeca, which declines like a feminine singular: G djece, D djeci.

items.en: part: genitive dijela, locative dijelu
items.note: The o was an l once — it comes back in the oblique cases: dio → dijela, dijelu, dijelom.

items.en: word: genitive riječi, instrumental riječju
items.note: The feminine i-declension: almost everything is -i, with the special instrumental riječju (or riječi).

items.en: colleague: genitive kolege, dative kolegi
items.note: Masculine in meaning, a-declension in form: kolege, kolegi, kolegu — but adjectives stay masculine: moj kolega.

items.en: I'm giving a piece of the cake to every child.
items.note: The graduation sentence: dio + genitive (kolača) + dative phrase (svakom djetetu). Three special patterns, one sentence.

activities.title: Case machine — speed round
activities.intro: Twenty seconds a question, tops. Full phrases through the cases, plus the special nouns that the milestone will definitely ask about.

questions.prompt: The genitive of 'moj najbolji prijatelj' is:
questions.explanation: Every word declines together: mog + najboljeg + prijatelja. The -om/-em/-u set is the dative.

questions.prompt: Genitiv od 'taksi': ___
questions.explanation: Foreign nouns in -i insert -j-: taksi → taksija. Instrumental: taksijem.

questions.prompt: Genitiv od 'ime': ___
questions.explanation: The -en- stem extension: ime → imena. Same as vrijeme → vremena.

questions.prompt: The genitive of 'dijete' is:
questions.explanation: Singular takes the -et- extension: djeteta. djece is the genitive of the plural djeca — a different word form entirely.

questions.prompt: Genitiv od 'dio': ___
questions.explanation: The hidden l returns: dio → dijela, dijelu, dijelom.

questions.prompt: Pišem ___ prijatelju. (my best — dative)
questions.explanation: Dative masculine: mom najboljem prijatelju. The -im/-im set is the instrumental.

questions.prompt: Putujem sa svojim najboljim ___. (prijatelj — instrumental)
questions.explanation: Instrumental after s/sa: prijateljem — soft stems (lj) take -em, not -om.

questions.prompt: Vidim ___ sestru u gradu. (my own)
questions.explanation: The subject (ja) owns her → svoj, and the feminine accusative is svoju sestru.

questions.prompt: Say: We talked about the names of our children.
questions.answer: Razgovarali smo o imenima naše djece
questions.explanation: Two special patterns at once: locative plural imenima (from ime), and djeca declining like a feminine singular: naše djece.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Dao sam dio kolača svakom djetetu.
Dao sam dijela kolač svakom djetetu.
Dao sam dio kolača svakom dijetu.
Dao sam dio kolač svakome djeteta.
questions.answer: Dao sam dio kolača svakom djetetu.
questions.explanation: dio stays accusative (= nominative), kolača is the genitive 'of cake', and dijete takes the -et- extension in the dative: djetetu.

days.title: Grammar sweep 2: clitics & aspect
days.objective: Produce correct clitic chains and aspect choices at conversation speed.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic order and aspect are the two A2 systems that most resist conscious control — a dedicated speed day pushes them toward automaticity.
drills: Do Unit 77 of A2.hr.
Rapid-fire: 10 sentences with li + dative + accusative clitics, spoken not written.
Aspect gauntlet: 15 prompts, pick and say the right verb of the pair instantly.
Tell one 60-second story with deliberate background/event aspect contrast.

items: Recite the clitic chain and give one maximal example.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give both aspects of 8 pairs, fast.

activities.title: Grammar sweep 2 — the clitic chain and the aspect switch
activities.intro: The two systems that resist conscious control: word order for the little words, and the perfective/imperfective choice. Read each line aloud twice — today is about speed, not theory.

items.hr: li → sam/ću/bih → mi/ti/joj → ga/je/ju → se → je
items.en: li → auxiliaries (except je) → dative → accusative/genitive → se → je
items.note: The full clitic chain, in stone. Everything sits in second position of the clause, in exactly this order.

items.en: Did you give it to me?
items.note: li after the stressed verb, then dative mi, then accusative ga. Chain in action.

items.en: I gave it to you yesterday.
items.note: Auxiliary sam first, dative ti second, accusative ga third — never ga ti.

items.en: I'll buy it for her tomorrow.
items.note: Future clitics obey the same chain: ću + joj (dative) + ga (accusative).

items.en: Ana got in touch with me last night.
items.note: Dative mi comes BEFORE se — the chain puts se after the object clitics.

items.en: He returned the book to her.
items.note: je is the outlaw: the auxiliary je goes LAST in the chain, after everything else — joj je, never je joj.

items.hr: kupovati / kupiti — Mjesecima sam kupovao auto; napokon sam ga kupio.
items.en: to be buying / to buy — For months I was car-shopping; I finally bought it.
items.note: Imperfective = the process; perfective = the completed event. One sentence, both jobs.

items.hr: pisati / napisati — Cijelo jutro sam pisao pismo i napokon ga napisao.
items.en: to be writing / to write (finish) — I wrote at the letter all morning and finally got it written.
items.note: na- is one of the commonest perfectivizers: pisati → napisati, učiti → naučiti, crtati → nacrtati.

items.hr: čitati / pročitati — Čitam roman. Pročitao sam ga za tri dana.
items.en: to read / to read through — I'm reading a novel. I finished it in three days.
items.note: The present tense of a perfective (pročitam) can't describe 'right now' — ongoing needs the imperfective čitam.

items.hr: davati / dati — Baka mi je uvijek davala savjete; jučer mi je dala najbolji.
items.en: to give (repeatedly) / to give (once) — Grandma always gave me advice; yesterday she gave me the best one.
items.note: Habits and repetition → imperfective (davala); a single event → perfective (dala).

items.hr: uzimati / uzeti — Svaki dan uzimam vitamine. Uzeo sam zadnju tabletu.
items.en: to take (regularly) / to take (once) — I take vitamins every day. I took the last pill.
items.note: svaki dan, često, obično → imperfective. jednom, jučer, napokon → usually perfective.

items.hr: Dok sam kuhao večeru, netko je pozvonio.
items.en: While I was cooking dinner, someone rang the doorbell.
items.note: The storytelling formula: imperfective background (kuhao) + perfective event (pozvonio). Tonight's 60-second story runs on this.

activities.title: Clitics and aspect at conversation speed
activities.intro: No thinking time budgeted: chain order, dative-before-accusative, the je-goes-last rule, and fifteen years of aspect in nine questions.

questions.prompt: Which question is in the correct order?
questions.explanation: li comes right after the stressed verb, then dative (mi) before accusative (ga).

questions.prompt: Jesi ___ ga vidio? (question particle)
questions.explanation: li is the first slot of the clitic chain — nothing squeezes in before it.

questions.prompt: Promise it: I'll buy it for her tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Chain order after the infinitive stem: ću (auxiliary) → joj (dative) → ga (accusative).

questions.prompt: Ana ___ je poslala poruku. (to him)
questions.explanation: Sending TO someone = dative clitic mu, and it stands before the auxiliary je (which always goes last).

questions.prompt: Jučer sam napokon ___ knjigu. (finished reading it)
questions.explanation: napokon signals completion → perfective pročitati in the perfekt: pročitao sam.

questions.prompt: Cijelo ljeto sam ___ taj roman, ali ga nisam završio. (čitati or pročitati — perfekt, ja, m.)
questions.explanation: cijelo ljeto = duration, and it wasn't finished → imperfective čitati: čitao sam.

questions.prompt: Svaki dan ___ vitamine. (uzimati / uzeti)
questions.explanation: svaki dan = a habit → imperfective present uzimam. The perfective uzmem can't describe a routine.

questions.prompt: Tell the story: While I was cooking dinner, someone rang the doorbell.
questions.answer: Dok sam kuhao večeru netko je pozvonio
questions.explanation: Imperfective background (Dok sam kuhao) + perfective event (netko je pozvonio) — the storytelling formula.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Ona mi je se javila jučer.
questions.explanation: Dative mi comes before se in the chain, and there's no auxiliary je in a se-perfekt with ona: javila se, not je se.

days.title: Grammar sweep 3: conditional, futur II, comparison
days.objective: Command the mood-and-comparison block without hesitation.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional, futur II with kad/ako/dok, and comparatives are your expressive upgrades this phase — the final sweep makes sure they survive contact with real conversation.
drills: Do Unit 78 of A2.hr.
Produce: 4 conditionals (one per meaning), 3 futur II clauses, 5 declined comparatives.
Translate 8 mixed English prompts ('I would…', 'when I get…', 'better than…') at speed.
One 90-second talk: 'Moj život za pet godina' using all three systems.

items: Say a kad-bih wish and a kad-budem plan back to back.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the irregular comparatives one last time.

activities.title: Grammar sweep 3 — would, will-have, better-than
activities.intro: The expressive block in one sweep: the conditional and its four jobs, futur II after kad/ako/dok, and the comparatives — including the irregular five and how they decline.

items.hr: kondicional: ja bih, ti bi, on bi, mi bismo, vi biste, oni bi
items.en: conditional: I would, you would, he would, we would, you (pl.) would, they would
items.note: The auxiliary paradigm. In speech everything blurs to bi, but writing keeps bih, bismo, biste.

items.en: I'd like a coffee, please.
items.note: Job 1 — polite requests. The verb htjeti isn't even needed: bih + the thing you want.

items.hr: Na tvom mjestu ne bih to kupio.
items.en: In your place I wouldn't buy that.
items.note: Job 2 — advice. na tvom mjestu = in your place, the classic advice opener.

items.hr: Kad bih imao više vremena, više bih čitao.
items.en: If I had more time, I would read more.
items.note: Job 3 — hypotheticals: kad + bih in the condition, bih again in the result. Both halves conditional.

items.hr: Htio bih jednog dana živjeti uz more.
items.en: I'd like to live by the sea one day.
items.note: Job 4 — wishes and dreams: htio bih + infinitive. jednog dana — the temporal genitive opener.

items.hr: Kad budem imao vremena, doći ću.
items.en: When I have time, I'll come.
items.note: Futur II: budem + participle for the REAL future in kad/ako/dok clauses. Compare: kad bih = if only (unreal).

items.hr: Ako bude padala kiša, ostat ćemo doma.
items.en: If it rains, we'll stay home.
items.note: ako + futur II (bude padala) in the condition; futur I (ostat ćemo) in the result.

items.hr: Dok budeš spavao, ja ću raditi.
items.en: While you sleep, I'll work.
items.note: dok + futur II for a future background action. The trio is complete: kad, ako, dok.

items.hr: dobar → bolji → najbolji; loš → gori → najgori
items.en: good → better → best; bad → worse → worst
items.note: Irregular pair number one. naj- + comparative always gives the superlative.

items.hr: velik → veći; malen → manji; dug → dulji
items.en: big → bigger; small → smaller; long → longer
items.note: The other irregulars worth owning. dulji has a twin duži — both are standard.

items.hr: Ovo je bolji plan od prošlog.
items.en: This is a better plan than the last one.
items.note: Comparison with od + genitive: bolji od prošlog. The nego alternative: bolji nego prošli.

items.hr: Razgovarao sam s najboljim učiteljem na svijetu.
items.en: I talked with the best teacher in the world.
items.note: Comparatives and superlatives DECLINE like adjectives: s najboljim učiteljem (instrumental). Tonight: 'Moj život za pet godina', using all three systems.

activities.title: Would, will-have, better-than — the drill
activities.intro: Four conditional jobs, three futur II triggers, five irregular comparatives. If you clear this without hesitation, Saturday's milestone holds no fear.

questions.prompt: The 'mi' form of the conditional auxiliary is:
questions.explanation: mi bismo, vi biste — the two forms that separate careful speakers from the universal bi.

questions.prompt: Ja ___ htio čašu vode. (conditional auxiliary)
questions.explanation: First person singular: ja bih. Polite wishes run on htio/htjela bih + noun or infinitive.

questions.prompt: Na tvom mjestu ne ___ to kupio. (advice — implied subject ja)
questions.explanation: 'In your place I wouldn't…' — the adviser is ja, so bih. budem belongs to futur II, a different system.

questions.prompt: Kad ___ imao vremena, nazvat ću te. (biti — futur II, ja)
questions.explanation: A real future plan → kad + budem + imao. (kad bih imao would turn it into an unreal wish.)

questions.prompt: Ako sutra ___ padala kiša, nećemo ići na izlet.
questions.explanation: ako-clauses about the future take futur II: ako bude padala. Futur I (će) is banned inside the ako-clause.

questions.prompt: The comparative of 'dobar' is:
questions.explanation: Fully irregular: dobar → bolji → najbolji. gori is the comparative of loš.

questions.prompt: Ovaj film je ___ od onog. (loš — comparative)
questions.explanation: loš → gori → najgori. Comparison attaches with od + genitive: gori od onog.

questions.prompt: Razgovarali smo o ___ planu. (bolji — locative)
questions.explanation: Comparatives decline like adjectives: o boljem planu (locative). boljim would be the instrumental.

questions.prompt: Ona pjeva ___ od mene. (dobro — comparative adverb)
questions.explanation: Adverbs compare too: dobro → bolje → najbolje. The neuter-looking form is the adverb.

questions.prompt: Make the plan: When I have time, I'll come to your place.
questions.answer: Kad budem imao vremena doći ću k tebi
questions.explanation: Futur II in the kad-clause (budem imao), futur I in the main clause (doći ću), and k + dative (tebi).

days.title: Dress rehearsal: four skills
days.objective: Simulate the A2 exam experience across listening, reading, writing and speaking.
days.paretoFocus: A timed four-skill rehearsal two days before the milestone finds the last soft spots while there's still time to patch them.
drills: Do Unit 79 of A2.hr.
Listening: 2 unit audios, one pass, questions answered.
Reading + writing: one Razgovarajte s nama! text, then a 100-word response, timed 20 minutes.
Speaking: 3-minute monologue on a surprise topic (pick blind from your theme list).

items: Review every error from today honestly.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Pick tomorrow's talk topic: your Croatian story so far.

activities.title: Dress-rehearsal toolkit — exam language and survival moves
activities.intro: Today you simulate all four skills under time pressure. Learn the instruction language you'll read on any test, plus the repair moves — paraphrasing, buying time, structuring — that keep a monologue alive.

items.en: How do you say 'deadline' in Croatian?
items.note: The number-one repair move: kako se kaže X. (Answer: rok.) Never let one missing word stop a sentence.

items.en: Could you repeat that, please?
items.note: Asking for repetition is allowed at every exam level — using it fluently is itself a skill.

items.hr: Nisam siguran, ali mislim da je tako.
items.en: I'm not sure, but I think that's how it is.
items.note: The hedge: nisam siguran/sigurna + ali mislim da. Keeps you talking when facts wobble.

items.en: In other words, it was too far.
items.note: drugim riječima = in other words — the paraphrase signal, instrumental plural of druga riječ.

items.hr: To je ono što koristimo kad pada kiša.
items.en: It's the thing we use when it rains.
items.note: The describe-around-it move: To je ono što… kad… Works for any noun you've forgotten (kišobran!).

items.en: Just a moment, I need to think.
items.note: Buying time out loud beats silence. Also: pa, znate…, kako da kažem… (how shall I put it…).

items.hr: Najprije ću govoriti o svom poslu, a zatim o planovima.
items.en: First I'll talk about my job, and then about my plans.
items.note: Announce your structure and the monologue organizes itself. najprije… zatim… na kraju…

items.hr: Da zaključim: bilo je teško, ali je vrijedilo.
items.en: To conclude: it was hard, but it was worth it.
items.note: da zaključim = to sum up — the landing phrase that ends a talk on purpose instead of trailing off.

items.hr: Pročitajte tekst i odgovorite na pitanja.
items.en: Read the text and answer the questions.
items.note: Exam instruction language: odgovoriti na + accusative (pitanja). You'll read this on every test forever.

items.en: Circle the correct answer.
items.note: zaokružiti = to circle; točan = correct. Its partner: dopunite rečenicu = complete the sentence.

items.en: Write a composition of one hundred words.
items.note: sastavak = composition, essay; od sto riječi — genitive plural riječi. Today's timed writing, named.

activities.title: Four skills under the clock
activities.intro: A miniature of Saturday's milestone: three listening items on one pass, a reading text, exam instructions and the survival phrases — timed head, calm hands.

questions.prompt: 'Možete li ponoviti?' means:
questions.explanation: ponoviti = to repeat. Slowing down is Možete li govoriti sporije?

questions.prompt: Listen to the port announcement once: what's the situation?
options: The Split ferry is 30 minutes late because of strong wind
The Split ferry is cancelled because of strong wind
The Split ferry leaves 30 minutes early
The Zadar ferry is 13 minutes late
questions.answer: The Split ferry is 30 minutes late because of strong wind
questions.explanation: Trajekt za Split kasni trideset minuta zbog jakog vjetra — late thirty minutes, cause introduced by zbog.
questions.audioText: Poštovani putnici, trajekt za Split kasni trideset minuta zbog jakog vjetra. Zahvaljujemo na razumijevanju.

questions.prompt: Listen to the voicemail: what changed?
options: The meeting moves from seven to eight, in front of the cinema
The meeting moves from eight to seven, in front of the cinema
The meeting is cancelled because of work
The meeting moves to the office at eight
questions.answer: The meeting moves from seven to eight, in front of the cinema
questions.explanation: Ne stižem u sedam nego u osam — not at seven but at eight, because of staying longer at work; meeting point: ispred kina.
questions.audioText: Bok, ja sam! Ne stižem u sedam nego u osam jer moram ostati dulje na poslu. Nađemo se ispred kina, može? Vidimo se!

questions.prompt: Listen to the radio ad: which statement is true?
options: Experience is not required for the waiter job
The restaurant is outside the city centre
The working hours are ten to eight in the evening
You should call in the morning
questions.answer: Experience is not required for the waiter job
questions.explanation: iskustvo nije potrebno = experience not needed. Hours are ten to eighteen (six p.m.), and you call weekday afternoons.
questions.audioText: Tražimo konobara za rad u restoranu u centru grada. Radno vrijeme je od deset do osamnaest sati, a iskustvo nije potrebno. Nazovite nas radnim danom poslije podne.

questions.prompt: Read: 'Marko je jučer izgubio novčanik u tramvaju. Danas ga je nazvala žena koja ga je našla. Sve je bilo unutra: novac, kartice i vozačka dozvola.' — What happened?
options: A woman found his wallet and everything was still inside
Marko found a woman's wallet in the tram
The wallet was returned but the money was gone
questions.answer: A woman found his wallet and everything was still inside
questions.explanation: žena koja ga je našla = the woman who found it; sve je bilo unutra = everything was inside. He lost it jučer, she called danas.

questions.prompt: Kako se ___ 'holiday' na hrvatskom? (reći — the se-form)
questions.explanation: kako se kaže = how does one say — your forever repair move. (Answer: praznik, or godišnji odmor for time off work.)

questions.prompt: Announce your monologue's structure: First I'll talk about my job.
questions.answer: Najprije ću govoriti o svom poslu
questions.explanation: Sequencer first, ću in second position, and svom because the job belongs to the subject.

questions.prompt: Napišite sastavak od sto ___. (riječ — after sto)
questions.explanation: Numbers from five up (and round hundreds) take the genitive plural: sto riječi.

questions.prompt: ___ siguran, ali mislim da je odgovor točan.
questions.explanation: The hedge is built on biti: nisam siguran = I'm not sure. Nemam negates imati, nije is third person.

questions.prompt: You forgot the word 'kišobran' mid-sentence. The best A2 move:
options: "To je ono što koristimo kad pada kiša."
Stop talking and wait for help.
questions.answer: "To je ono što koristimo kad pada kiša."
questions.explanation: Describing around a missing word keeps the conversation yours — and it's exactly what examiners reward at A2.

days.title: Unit 80 — the e-course is done!
days.objective: Finish the final unit of E-tečaj A2.hr and deliver a 5-minute talk.
days.paretoFocus: Eighty units, eight modules, ninety days. Closing the course with a long free talk proves the knowledge left the screen and moved into you.
drills: Do Unit 80 of A2.hr — Module 8 complete, ALL 80 UNITS DONE!
Deliver and record a 5-minute talk: your life, your plans, your Croatian journey.
Watch tonight's HRT news and note how much you now follow.
Write one sentence to your day-61 self — in Croatian, obviously.

items: Listen to your talk and mark 3 wins and 3 fixes.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Prepare your can-do checklist for tomorrow's milestone.

activities.title: Osamdeset lekcija poslije — words for the journey
activities.intro: The course is done today. Here is the vocabulary of looking back and pushing forward — progress, habit, pride, goals — plus the frames for your recorded five-minute talk.

items.hr: Danas sam završio cijeli tečaj — svih osamdeset lekcija!
items.en: Today I finished the whole course — all eighty lessons!
items.note: završiti = to finish (perfective, of course — it's DONE); svih osamdeset + genitive plural lekcija.

items.en: I'm proud of my progress.
items.note: ponosan na + accusative; napredak = progress, with a fleeting a: genitive napretka.

items.hr: Prije devedeset dana nisam znao ni naručiti kavu.
items.en: Ninety days ago I couldn't even order a coffee.
items.note: prije + genitive = ago; ni = not even. The before-photo of your progress story.

items.hr: Učenje jezika je maraton, a ne sprint.
items.en: Learning a language is a marathon, not a sprint.
items.note: učenje = learning, a verbal noun from učiti. The sentence every learner earns the right to say around day 149.

items.en: Habit is more important than motivation.
items.note: navika = habit — your real teacher these ninety days. Comparative + od + genitive: važnija od motivacije.

items.en: Every day I learn something new.
items.note: Temporal genitive svakog dana + perfective naučim: each day produces one completed act of learning.

items.hr: Moja najveća pobjeda: razumijem svoju punicu!
items.en: My biggest victory: I understand my mother-in-law!
items.note: pobjeda = victory; punica = your wife's mother. The most honest can-do statement in this app.

items.hr: Zahvalan sam svojoj ženi na strpljenju.
items.en: I'm grateful to my wife for her patience.
items.note: zahvalan + dative (svojoj ženi) + na + locative (strpljenju). Say it to her tonight, not just to the app.

items.hr: Cilj za sljedeću fazu: govoriti tečnije.
items.en: The goal for the next phase: to speak more fluently.
items.note: cilj = goal; tečno = fluently → comparative tečnije. B1 in one word.

items.hr: Poruka mom starom ja: samo nastavi, isplati se!
items.en: A message to my old self: just keep going, it's worth it!
items.note: isplatiti se = to pay off, be worth it. Today's drill: one sentence to your day-61 self — this is the template.

items.hr: Sada mogu ispričati svoju priču na hrvatskom.
items.en: Now I can tell my story in Croatian.
items.note: ispričati = to tell (a whole story, perfective). Your five-minute talk today is exactly that — record it.

activities.title: The journey, declined and conjugated
activities.intro: The vocabulary of progress doubles as a grammar review: fleeting a in napredak, na + locative after zahvalan, comparatives of adverbs. One warm-up before the big talk.

questions.prompt: What does 'napredak' mean?
questions.explanation: napredak = progress. Habit is navika, goal is cilj, victory is pobjeda.

questions.prompt: Ponosan sam na svoj ___. (progress)
questions.explanation: ponosan na + accusative, and napredak (inanimate) keeps its nominative shape.

questions.prompt: The genitive of 'napredak' is:
questions.explanation: The fleeting a drops AND d assimilates to t before k: napredak → napretka. Two sound rules in one word.

questions.prompt: Zahvalan sam svojoj ženi na ___. (strpljenje — after na)
questions.explanation: na + locative: strpljenje → strpljenju. The person you thank goes in the dative: svojoj ženi.

questions.prompt: Prije devedeset ___ počeo sam ovu fazu. (dan)
questions.explanation: prije + genitive, and devedeset takes the genitive plural: devedeset dana = ninety days ago.

questions.prompt: State the habit that got you here: Every day I learn something new.
questions.explanation: Temporal genitive opener (svakog dana), then perfective naučim — one completed bite of learning per day.

questions.prompt: Cilj mi je govoriti ___. (tečno — comparative)
questions.explanation: Adverb comparison: tečno → tečnije. cilj mi je + infinitive = my goal is to…

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Danas sam završio svih osamdeset lekcija.
Danas sam završio sve osamdeset lekcije.
Danas sam završavao svih osamdeset lekcija.
questions.answer: Danas sam završio svih osamdeset lekcija.
questions.explanation: Perfective perfekt for the completed triumph (sam završio), and svih osamdeset + genitive plural lekcija.

activities.title: The last lesson — a toast before the milestone
activities.intro: The course is finished and someone at home has heard every step of it. Tonight: the look back, the five-minute talk report, and the eve of tomorrow's milestone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: I? Reci mi! Jesi li završio?
lines.en: So? Tell me! Did you finish?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam! Osamdeset lekcija — gotovo! Upravo sam završio zadnju.
lines.en: I did! Eighty lessons — done! I just finished the last one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ponosna sam na tebe. Sjećaš li se kako je bilo na početku?
lines.en: I'm proud of you. Do you remember what it was like at the beginning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sjećam se. Prije tri mjeseca nisam znao razliku između 'kupiti' i 'kupovati'.
lines.en: I remember. Three months ago I didn't know the difference between 'kupiti' and 'kupovati'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sad mi pričaš viceve na hrvatskom. Kako si izdržao svaki dan?
lines.en: And now you tell me jokes in Croatian. How did you keep it up every day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Navika. Svakog dana jedna lekcija, bez izgovora. I tvoja pomoć, naravno.
lines.en: Habit. One lesson every day, no excuses. And your help, of course.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što ti je bilo najteže?
lines.en: What was hardest for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na početku padeži, a kasnije glagolski vid. Ali najviše mi je pomoglo to što kod kuće govorimo hrvatski.
lines.en: At the beginning the cases, and later verbal aspect. But what helped me most is that we speak Croatian at home.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Danas si snimio i onaj dugi govor. Kako je prošlo?
lines.en: Today you also recorded that long talk. How did it go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Govorio sam pet minuta bez prestanka — o životu, o planovima, o nama. Poslušat ću snimku i zapisati tri pobjede i tri greške.
lines.en: I spoke for five minutes without stopping — about life, plans, us. I'll listen to the recording and note three wins and three mistakes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sutra je velika provjera. Jesi li spreman?
lines.en: Tomorrow is the big assessment. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mislim da jesam. A ako nešto ne budem znao, naučit ću to na putu prema B1.
lines.en: I think I am. And if there's something I don't know, I'll learn it on the road to B1.

days.title: A2 milestone: can-dos, quiz and the B1 plan
days.objective: Confirm A2 against the official can-dos, pass the A2 quiz, and lay out your B1 phase.
days.paretoFocus: A milestone measured honestly is worth a month of drifting study. You can now shop, arrange, apologize, narrate and describe — certify it, celebrate it, and aim the habit at B1.
drills: Self-assess against the A2 can-dos: everyday transactions, arranging meetings, describing people/home/work/past events, short letters and messages.
Take the A2 quiz in the app and complete any open A2 Feynman concepts.
Have a celebratory 20-minute conversation — tutor or your Croatian spouse, who has heard every step of this.
Plan the B1 phase: Razgovarajte s nama! B1, daily HRT news, weekly conversation sessions with your partner, and the B1 grammar ladder in the app.

items: Tick off every A2 can-do statement in writing.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Celebrate — 150 days, A2 reached, B1 in sight!

activities.title: A2, potvrđeno — your can-dos and the B1 plan
activities.intro: Milestone day. First, the official can-dos in your own Croatian — read each one aloud and honestly tick it. Then the vocabulary of the next phase, because the plan you write today is the B1 you get.

items.hr: Mogu naručiti hranu i piće te platiti račun.
items.en: I can order food and drink and pay the bill.
items.note: Can-do #1: everyday transactions. te = and (formal-ish connector between verb phrases).
